vf.rangeInfo,\n121\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n122\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n123\t\t}\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\t// =============================================================================\n127\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n128\t// =============================================================================\n129\t\n130\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n131\t//\n132\t// Parameters:\n133\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n134\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n135\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n136\t//\n137\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n138\t//\n139\t// Example usage:\n140\t//\n141\t//\terr := Found)\n146\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n147\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n148\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n149\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n150\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n151\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n152\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n153\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n154\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n155\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n156\t\t\tFormat()\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n160\t//\n161\t// Parameters:\n162\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n163\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n164\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n165\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n166\t//\n167\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n168\t//\n169\t// Example usage:\n170\t//\n171\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n172\t//\t// Output:\n173\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n174\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n175\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n176\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n177\t//\t//   Field:    error\n178\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n179\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n180\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n181\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n182\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n184\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n185\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n186\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n187\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n188\t\t\tFormat()\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n192\t//\n193\t// Parameters:\n194\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n195\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n196\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n197\t//\n198\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n199\t//\n200\t// Example usage:\n201\t//\n202\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n203\t//\t// Output:\n204\t//\t// status_code_range parameters for errors\n210\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n211\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n212\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n213\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n214\t\n215\t\t// Build range info string\n216\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n217\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n218\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n219\t\t}\n220\t\n221\t\tdetails := []string{}\n222\t\tif err != nil {\n223\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n224\t\t} else {\n225\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n229\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, {\n236\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type configuration\"),\n292\t//\t)\n293\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n294\t\tvalidationType string,\n295\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n296\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n297\t) ValidationError {\n298\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n299\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n300\t\t\topt(config)\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n304\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n305\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n306\t\n307\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n308\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n318\t\t}\n319\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n320\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n321\t\t}\n322\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n323\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n324\t\t}\n325\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n326\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n327\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\t// =============================================================================\n335\t\n336\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n337\ttype FormatConfig struct nil\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t// =============================================================================\n420\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n421\t// =============================================================================\n422\t\n423\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n424\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n425\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n426\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n427\t//\n428\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n429\t// and ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification. use fallback\n464\t\tif message == \"\" {\n465\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n466\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n467\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n468\t\t\t} else {\n469\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n470\t\t\t}\n471\t\t}\n472\t\n473\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage which is already integrated with ErrorType\n474\t\t// This ensures consistent error classification across all errors\n475\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n479\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n480\t// instead of string-based error types.\n481\t//\n482\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n483\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n484\t//\n485\t// Parameters:\n486\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n487\t//   - message: The error message content\n488\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n489\t//\n490\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n491\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n492\t//\n493\t// Example usage:\n494\t//\n495\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n496\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n497\t//\n498\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n499\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n500\t//\n501\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n502\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n503\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n504\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n505\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n506\t\n507\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n508\t\tif message == \"\" {\n509\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n510\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n511\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n512\t\t\t} else {\n513\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n514\t\t\t}\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n518\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n519\t}\n520\t\n521\t// =============================================================================\n522\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n523\t// =============================================================================\n524\t\n525\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n526\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n527\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n528\t//\n529\t// Parameters:\n530\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n531\t//\n532\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n533\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n534\t//\n535\t// Example usage:\n536\t//\n537\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n538\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n539\t//\n540\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n541\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n542\t//\n543\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n544\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n545\t//\n546\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n547\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n548\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n549\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n550\t\n551\t\t// Handle empty path\n552\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n553\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n557\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n558\t}\n559\t\n560\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n561\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n562\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n563\t\tif path == \"\" {\n564\t\t\treturn \"\"\n565\t\t}\n566\t\n567\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n568\t\tvar result []string\n569\t\n570\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n571\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n572\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n573\t\t\t\tcontinue\n574\t\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n577\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n578\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n579\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n580\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n581\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n582\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n583\t\t\t\t} else {\n584\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n585\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n586\t\t\t\t}\n587\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n588\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n589\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n590\t\t\t} else {\n591\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n592\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n593\t\t\t}\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n597\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n598\t\t}\n599\t\n600\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n601\t}\n602\t\n603\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n604\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n605\t\tif s == \"\" {\n606\t\t\treturn false\n607\t\t}\n608\t\n609\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n610\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n611\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n612\t\t\t\treturn false\n613\t\t\t}\n614\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n615\t\t}\n616\t\n617\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n618\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n619\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n620\t\t\t\treturn false\n621\t\t\t}\n622\t\t}\n623\t\n624\t\treturn true\n625\t}\n626\t\n627\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n628\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n629\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n630\t}\n631\t\n632\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_b123bd7a851d44c2b5a16689","session_id":"8e75914e-0a81-42bf-8c89-c7bd0a7a6a80","uuid":"b9a6a9e6-931b-450e-a2a6-5b08d7dc3ecf","timestamp":"2026-07-14T14:32:20.606Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find FormatError and ErrorType definitions"}

=============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY =============================================================================\n470\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n471\t// =============================================================================\n472\t\n473\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n474\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n475\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n476\t//\n477\t// Parameters:\n478\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n479\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n480\t//\n481\t// Returns a formatted error FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n494\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n495\t\n496\t\t// Add severity if requested\n497\t\tif includeSeverity {\n498\t\t\t// {\n502\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n503\t\t\t} else {\n504\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n505\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n506\t\t\t}\n507\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Add error type and field\n511\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n512\t\n513\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n514\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n515\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n516\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n518\t\t\t}\n519\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n520\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n521\t\t\t}\n522\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n523\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n524\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t// Add location if present\n529\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n530\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\treturn builder.String()\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n537\t// This is useful for summaries and =============================================================================\n563\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n564\t// =============================================================================\n565\t\n566\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n567\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n568\t//\n569\t// Parameters:\n570\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n571\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n591\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n592\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n593\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\tvar lines []string\n597\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n598\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n599\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n600\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n607\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n608\t//\n609\t// Parameters:\n610\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n611\t//\n612\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n613\t//\n614\t// Example usage:\n615\t//\n616\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n617\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n618\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n619\t//\t}, {\n620\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n621\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n622\t//\t}}\n623\t//\n624\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n625\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n626\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n627\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n628\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n629\t\t}\n630\t\n631\t\t// Count errors by type\n632\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n633\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n634\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\t// Build summary parts\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\t// =============================================================================\n647\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n648\t// =============================================================================\n649\t\n650\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n651\t// without the parent parameter.\n652\t//\n653\t// Parameters:\n654\t//   - Access-Control-Max-Age.\n320\t\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n321\t\tMaxAge string\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n325\t// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n326\t//\n327\t// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n328\t// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n329\t//\n330\t// Parameters:\n331\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n332\t//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n333\t//\n334\t// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n335\t// Returns false if the response is nil.\n336\t//\n337\t// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n338\t// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n339\t//\n340\t// Example usage:\n341\t//\n342\t//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n343\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n344\t//\t    // CORS headers are present\n345\t//\t}\n346\t//\n347\t//\t// Validate specific origin\n348\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n349\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n350\t//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n351\t//\t}\n352\t//\n353\t//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n354\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n355\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n356\t//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n357\t//\t}\n358\t//\n359\t//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n360\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n361\t//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n362\t//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n363\t//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n364\t//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n365\t//\t}\n366\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n367\t//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n368\t//\t}\n369\tfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n370\t\tif resp == nil {\n371\t\t\treturn false\n372\t\t}\n373\t\n374\t\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n375\t\tif config == nil {\n376\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n377\t\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n378\t\t}\n379\t\n380\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n381\t\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n382\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n383\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n384\t\t\t\treturn false\n385\t\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n388\t\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n389\t\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n390\t\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n391\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n392\t\t\t\t}\n393\t\t\t} else {\n394\t\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n395\t\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n396\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n397\t\t\t\t}\n398\t\t\t}\n399\t\t}\n400\t\n401\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n402\t\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n403\t\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n404\t\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n405\t\t\t\treturn false\n406\t\t\t}\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n410\t\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n411\t\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n412\t\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n413\t\t\t\treturn false\n414\t\t\t}\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n418\t\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n419\t\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n420\t\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n421\t\t\t\treturn false\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n426\t\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n427\t\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n428\t\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n429\t\t\t\treturn false\n430\t\t\t}\n431\t\t}\n432\t\n433\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n434\t\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n435\t\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n436\t\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n437\t\t\t\treturn false\n438\t\t\t}\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn true\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// =============================================================================\n445\t// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n446\t// =============================================================================\n447\t\n448\t// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\n449\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n450\t\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n451\t\tPattern string\n452\t\n453\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n454\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n455\t\n456\t\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n457\t\tMatchAny bool\n458\t\n459\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n460\t\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n461\t\tFieldNames []string\n462\t}\n463\t\n464\t// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n465\t//\n466\t// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n467\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n468\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n469\t//\n470\t// Parameters:\n471\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n472\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n473\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n474\t//\n475\t// Returns:\n476\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n477\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n478\t//\n479\t// Example usage:\n480\t//\n481\t//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n482\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n483\t//\n484\t//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n485\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\n486\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n487\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n488\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\tif pattern == \"\" {\n492\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n493\t\t}\n494\t\n495\t\t// Parse JSON body\n496\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n497\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n498\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n499\t\t}\n500\t\n501\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n502\t\tregexPattern := pattern\n503\t\tif caseInsensitive {\n504\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n505\t\t}\n506\t\n507\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n508\t\tif err != nil {\n509\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n510\t\t}\n511\t\n512\t\t// Check default error fields\n513\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n514\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n515\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n516\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n517\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n518\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n519\t\t\t\t\t}\n520\t\t\t\t}\n521\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n522\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n523\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n524\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n525\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n526\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n527\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n528\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t\t\t}\n530\t\t\t\t}\n531\t\t\t}\n532\t\t}\n533\t\n534\t\treturn false, nil\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n538\t//\n539\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n540\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n541\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n542\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n543\t//\n544\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n545\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n546\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n547\t//\n548\t// Parameters:\n549\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n550\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n551\t//\n552\t// Returns:\n553\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n554\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n555\t//\n556\t// Example usage:\n557\t//\n558\t//\t// Regex pattern matching\n559\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n560\t//\tif err != nil {\n561\t//\t    // Pattern not found\n562\t//\t}\n563\t//\n564\t//\t// Simple substring matching\n565\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n566\t//\tif err != nil {\n567\t//\t    // Substring not found\n568\t//\t}\n569\t//\n570\t//\t// Common error patterns\n571\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n572\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n573\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n574\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n575\t//\n576\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n577\t//\n578\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n579\t//\n580\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n581\t//\n582\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n583\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n584\t//\n585\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n586\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n587\t//\n588\t// # Authentication Errors\n589\t//\n590\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n591\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n592\t//\n593\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n594\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n595\t//\n596\t// # Authorization Errors\n597\t//\n598\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n599\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n600\t//\n601\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n602\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n603\t//\n604\t// # Validation Errors\n605\t//\n606\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n607\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"validation.*failed\")  // regex match\n608\t//\n609\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\"}`)\n610\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*input\")  // regex match\n611\t//\n612\t// # Resource Errors\n613\t//\n614\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Resource not found\"}`)\n615\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not found\")  // substring match\n616\t//\n617\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"User does not exist\"}`)\n618\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"does not exist\")  // substring match\n619\t//\n620\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\n621\t//\n622\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}`)\n623\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"rate.*limit\")  // regex match\n624\t//\n625\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Too many requests, please try again later\"}`)\n626\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"too many requests\")  // substring match\n627\t//\n628\t// # Timeout Errors\n629\t//\n630\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Request timeout\"}`)\n631\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n632\t//\n633\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\"}`)\n634\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n635\t//\n636\t// # Server Errors\n637\t//\n638\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}`)\n639\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"internal.*error\")  // regex match\n640\t//\n641\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Service temporarily unavailable\"}`)\n642\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"unavailable\")  // substring match\n643\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\n644\t\t// Validate inputs\n645\t\tif len(response) == 0 {\n646\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n647\t\t}\n648\t\n649\t\tif expectedPattern == \"\" {\n650\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected pattern cannot be empty\")\n651\t\t}\n652\t\n653\t\t// Parse JSON body\n654\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n655\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\n656\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\t// Search for error messages in common fields\n660\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n661\t\tvar foundMessages []string\n662\t\n663\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n664\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n665\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n666\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\n667\t\t\t\t}\n668\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n669\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n670\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n671\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n672\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\n673\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n674\t\t\t\t\t}\n675\t\t\t\t}\n676\t\t\t}\n677\t\t}\n678\t\n679\t\t// If no error messages found, return error with response snippet\n680\t\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\n681\t\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n682\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no error message found in response body. Response snippet: %s\", snippet)\n683\t\t}\n684\t\n685\t\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\n686\t\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\n687\t\n688\t\t// Check each found message for pattern match\n689\t\tfor _, message := range foundMessages {\n690\t\t\tvar matched bool\n691\t\n692\t\t\tif isRegex {\n693\t\t\t\t// Try regex matching\n694\t\t\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\n695\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n696\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern '%s': %w\", expectedPattern, err)\n697\t\t\t\t}\n698\t\t\t\tmatched = re.MatchString(message)\n699\t\t\t} else {\n700\t\t\t\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\n701\t\t\t\tmatched = strings.Contains(message, expectedPattern)\n702\t\t\t}\n703\t\n704\t\t\tif matched {\n705\t\t\t\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\n706\t\t\t\treturn nil\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t}\n709\t\n710\t\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error\n711\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n712\t\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\n713\t\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\n714\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pattern '%s' not found in error messages. First message: \\\"%s\\\". Response snippet: %s\",\n715\t\t\t\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\n716\t\t}\n717\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pattern '%s' not found in error message: \\\"%s\\\". Response snippet: %s\",\n718\t\t\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\n719\t}\n720\t\n721\t// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\n722\t// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\n723\tfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\n724\t\tregexChars := []string{\".\", \"*\", \"+\", \"?\", \"^\", \"$\", \"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \"(\", \")\", \"|\", \"\\\\\"}\n725\t\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\n726\t\t\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\n727\t\t\t\treturn true\n728\t\t\t}\n729\t\t}\n730\t\treturn false\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\n734\t// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\n735\tfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\n736\t\t// Convert to string and truncate\n737\t\tresponseStr := string(response)\n738\t\tmaxLen := 200\n739\t\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\n740\t\t\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \"...\"\n741\t\t}\n742\t\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\n743\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\n\", \" \")\n744\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\r\", \" \")\n745\t\treturn responseStr\n746\t}\n747\t\n748\t// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\n749\t//\n750\t// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\n751\t// - Custom field selection\n752\t// - Case-insensitive matching\n753\t// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\n754\t// - Multiple pattern checking\n755\t//\n756\t// Parameters:\n757\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n758\t//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\n759\t//\n760\t// Returns:\n761\t//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\n762\t//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\n763\t//\n764\t// Example usage:\n765\t//\n766\t//\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\n767\t//\t    Pattern:         \"unauthorized\",\n768\t//\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\n769\t//\t    FieldNames:      []string{\"error\", \"detail\"},\n770\t//\t}\n771\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\n772\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\n773\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n774\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n775\t\t}\n776\t\n777\t\tif config == nil {\n778\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"config cannot be nil\")\n779\t\t}\n780\t\n781\t\tif config.Pattern == \"\" {\n782\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n783\t\t}\n784\t\n785\t\t// Parse JSON body\n786\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n787\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n788\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n789\t\t}\n790\t\n791\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n792\t\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\n793\t\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\n794\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + config.Pattern\n795\t\t}\n796\t\n797\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n798\t\tif err != nil {\n799\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n800\t\t}\n801\t\n802\t\t// Determine which fields to check\n803\t\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\n804\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n805\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n806\t\t}\n807\t\n808\t\t// Check each specified field\n809\t\tmatchedFields := []string{}\n810\t\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\n811\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n812\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n813\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n814\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n815\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n816\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n817\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n818\t\t\t\t\t}\n819\t\t\t\t}\n820\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n821\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n822\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n823\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n824\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n825\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n826\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n827\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n828\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n829\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n830\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n831\t\t\t\t\t}\n832\t\t\t\t}\n833\t\t\t}\n834\t\t}\n835\t\n836\t\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\n837\t\tif !config.MatchAny {\n838\t\t\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\n839\t\t}\n840\t\n841\t\treturn false, nil\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\t// =============================================================================\n845\t// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\n846\t// =============================================================================\n847\t\n848\t// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\n849\t//\n850\t// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\n851\t// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\n852\t//\n853\t// Parameters:\n854\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n855\t//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"INVALID_TOKEN\", \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n856\t//\n857\t// Returns:\n858\t//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\n859\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n860\t//\n861\t// Example usage:\n862\t//\n863\t//\t// Check for \"AUTH_FAILED\" error code\n864\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n865\t//\n866\t//\t// Check for OAuth \"invalid_token\" error\n867\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\")\n868\tfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n869\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n870\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n871\t\t}\n872\t\n873\t\tif expectedCode == \"\" {\n874\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"expected code cannot be empty\")\n875\t\t}\n876\t\n877\t\t// Parse JSON body\n878\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n879\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n880\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n881\t\t}\n882\t\n883\t\t// Common error code field names\n884\t\terrorCodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n885\t\n886\t\t// Check for error code in standard fields\n887\t\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\n888\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n889\t\t\t\t// Check for string match\n890\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n891\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n892\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n893\t\t\t\t\t}\n894\t\t\t\t}\n895\t\t\t\t// Check for nested error code in error object\n896\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n897\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n898\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok {\n899\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n900\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n901\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n902\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n903\t\t\t\t\t}\n904\t\t\t\t}\n905\t\t\t}\n906\t\t}\n907\t\n908\t\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\n909\t\tif errorCode, exists := body[\"error\"]; exists {\n910\t\t\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\n911\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n912\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n913\t\t\t\t}\n914\t\t\t}\n915\t\t}\n916\t\n917\t\treturn false, nil\n918\t}\n919\t\n920\t// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\n921\t//\n922\t// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\n923\t// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\n924\t// information are consistent.\n925\t//\n926\t// Parameters:\n927\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n928\t//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP status code\n929\t//   - expectedCode: Expected error code in response body\n930\t//\n931\t// Returns:\n932\t//   - true if both status code and error code match, false otherwise\n933\t//   - error if body parsing fails\n934\t//\n935\t// Example usage:\n936\t//\n937\t//\t// Validate 401 response with \"UNAUTHORIZED\" error code\n938\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 401, \"UNAUTHORIZED\")\n939\t//\n940\t//\t// Validate 403 response with \"FORBIDDEN\" error code\n941\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 403, \"FORBIDDEN\")\n942\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n943\t\tif resp == nil {\n944\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response is nil\")\n945\t\t}\n946\t\n947\t\t// Check status code\n948\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n949\t\t\treturn false, nil\n950\t\t}\n951\t\n952\t\t// Read response body\n953\t\tvar bodyBytes []byte\n954\t\tif resp.Body != nil {\n955\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n956\t\t\tvar err error\n957\t\t\tbodyBytes, err = io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n958\t\t\tif err != nil {\n959\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n960\t\t\t}\n961\t\t}\n962\t\n963\t\t// Check error code\n964\t\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\n965\t\tif err != nil {\n966\t\t\treturn false, err\n967\t\t}\n968\t\n969\t\treturn found, nil\n970\t}\n971\t\n972\t// GetErrorMessage extracts the error message from a response body.\n973\t//\n974\t// This helper function searches for error messages in common fields and returns\n975\t// the first non-empty message found.\n976\t//\n977\t// Parameters:\n978\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n979\t//\n980\t// Returns:\n981\t//   - The extracted error message, or empty string if not found\n982\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n983\t//\n984\t// Example usage:\n985\t//\n986\t//\tmessage, err := GetErrorMessage(body)\n987\t//\tif message != \"\" {\n988\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error: %s\\n\", message)\n989\t//\t}\n990\tfunc GetErrorMessage(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n991\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n992\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n993\t\t}\n994\t\n995\t\t// Parse JSON body\n996\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n997\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n998\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n999\t\t}\n1000\t\n1001\t\t// Check common message fields\n1002\t\tmessageFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n1003\t\tfor _, field := range messageFields {\n1004\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1005\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1006\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1007\t\t\t\t}\n1008\t\t\t\t// Check nested message in error object\n1009\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1010\t\t\t\t\tif messageValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n1011\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := messageValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1012\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1013\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1014\t\t\t\t\t}\n1015\t\t\t\t}\n1016\t\t\t}\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1020\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t// GetErrorCode extracts the error code from a response body.\n1023\t//\n1024\t// This helper function searches for error codes in common fields and returns\n1025\t// the first non-empty code found.\n1026\t//\n1027\t// Parameters:\n1028\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1029\t//\n1030\t// Returns:\n1031\t//   - The extracted error code, or empty string if not found\n1032\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1033\t//\n1034\t// Example usage:\n1035\t//\n1036\t//\tcode, err := GetErrorCode(body)\n1037\t//\tif code != \"\" {\n1038\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error code: %s\\n\", code)\n1039\t//\t}\n1040\tfunc GetErrorCode(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n1041\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1042\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1043\t\t}\n1044\t\n1045\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1046\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1047\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1048\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1049\t\t}\n1050\t\n1051\t\t// Check common code fields\n1052\t\tcodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\"}\n1053\t\tfor _, field := range codeFields {\n1054\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1055\t\t\t\t// Direct string code\n1056\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1057\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1058\t\t\t\t}\n1059\t\t\t\t// Check nested code in error object\n1060\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1061\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n1062\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1063\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1064\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1065\t\t\t\t\t}\n1066\t\t\t\t}\n1067\t\t\t}\n1068\t\t}\n1069\t\n1070\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1071\t}\n1072\t\n1073\t// =============================================================================\n1074\t// DETAILED VALIDATION RESULTS\n1075\t// =============================================================================\n1076\t\n1077\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\n1078\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\n1079\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\n1080\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n1081\t\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n1082\t\tValid bool\n1083\t\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n1084\t\tActualCode int\n1085\t\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\n1086\t\tExpectedCodes []int\n1087\t\t// MatchedCode is the specific expected code that matched (if any)\n1088\t\tMatchedCode *int\n1089\t\t// MismatchDetails contains human-readable information about why validation failed\n1090\t\tMismatchDetails string\n1091\t\t// IsClientError indicates whether the actual code is a client error (4xx)\n1092\t\tIsClientError bool\n1093\t\t// IsServerError indicates whether the actual code is a server error (5xx)\n1094\t\tIsServerError bool\n1095\t\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\n1096\t\tCategory string\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\n1100\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\n1101\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\n1102\t//\n1103\t// Parameters:\n1104\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1105\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\n1106\t//\n1107\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1108\t//\n1109\t// Example usage:\n1110\t//\n1111\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n1112\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1113\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %s\", result.MismatchDetails)\n1114\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Got: %d, expected one of: %v\", result.ActualCode, result.ExpectedCodes)\n1115\t//\t}\n1116\t//\n1117\t//\t// Single code validation\n1118\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, 200)\n1119\t//\tif result.Valid {\n1120\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code is valid: %d\", result.ActualCode)\n1121\t//\t}\n1122\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1123\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1124\t\t\tValid:             false,\n1125\t\t\tActualCode:        0,\n1126\t\t\tExpectedCodes:     []int{},\n1127\t\t\tMatchedCode:       nil,\n1128\t\t\tMismatchDetails:   \"\",\n1129\t\t\tIsClientError:     false,\n1130\t\t\tIsServerError:     false,\n1131\t\t\tCategory:          \"unknown\",\n1132\t\t}\n1133\t\n1134\t\tif resp == nil {\n1135\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1136\t\t\treturn result\n1137\t\t}\n1138\t\n1139\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1140\t\n1141\t\t// Set error flags and category\n1142\t\tswitch {\n1143\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500:\n1144\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1145\t\t\tresult.Category = \"client_error\"\n1146\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600:\n1147\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1148\t\t\tresult.Category = \"server_error\"\n1149\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300:\n1150\t\t\tresult.Category = \"success\"\n1151\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 300 && resp.StatusCode < 400:\n1152\t\t\tresult.Category = \"redirection\"\n1153\t\tdefault:\n1154\t\t\tresult.Category = \"other\"\n1155\t\t}\n1156\t\n1157\t\t// Parse expected codes\n1158\t\tvar expectedCodes []int\n1159\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n1160\t\tcase int:\n1161\t\t\texpectedCodes = []int{v}\n1162\t\tcase []int:\n1163\t\t\texpectedCodes = v\n1164\t\tdefault:\n1165\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid expected type: %T\", expected)\n1166\t\t\treturn result\n1167\t\t}\n1168\t\n1169\t\tresult.ExpectedCodes = expectedCodes\n1170\t\n1171\t\t// Check for match\n1172\t\tfor _, code := range expectedCodes {\n1173\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == code {\n1174\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = true\n1175\t\t\t\tresult.MatchedCode = &code\n1176\t\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"\"\n1177\t\t\t\treturn result\n1178\t\t\t}\n1179\t\t}\n1180\t\n1181\t\t// Build detailed mismatch information\n1182\t\tif len(expectedCodes) == 1 {\n1183\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"expected status code %d but got %d\", expectedCodes[0], resp.StatusCode)\n1184\t\t} else {\n1185\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"expected one of status codes %v but got %d\", expectedCodes, resp.StatusCode)\n1186\t\t}\n1187\t\n1188\t\treturn result\n1189\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t// StatusCodeRange defines a range of status codes for flexible validation.\n1192\t// This is useful when you want to accept any status code within a certain range.\n1193\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n1194\t\t// Min is the minimum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1195\t\tMin int\n1196\t\t// Max is the maximum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1197\t\tMax int\n1198\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of what this range represents\n1199\t\tDescription string\n1200\t}\n1201\t\n1202\t// Common status code ranges\n1203\tvar (\n1204\t\t// Range1xx covers informational responses (100-199)\n1205\t\tRange1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n1206\t\t// Range2xx covers successful responses (200-299)\n1207\t\tRange2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n1208\t\t// Range3xx covers redirection responses (300-399)\n1209\t\tRange3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n1210\t\t// Range4xx covers client error responses (400-499)\n1211\t\tRange4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n1212\t\t// Range5xx covers server error responses (500-599)\n1213\t\tRange5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n1214\t)\n1215\t\n1216\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange checks if a response's status code falls within a specified range.\n1217\t// This is useful for flexible validation where you want to accept any status code within a category.\n1218\t//\n1219\t// Parameters:\n1220\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1221\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1222\t//\n1223\t// Returns true if the response status code falls within the range, false otherwise.\n1224\t// Returns false if the response is nil or the range is invalid (Min > Max).\n1225\t//\n1226\t// Example usage:\n1227\t//\n1228\t//\t// Accept any success code (2xx)\n1229\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range2xx) {\n1230\t//\t    // Response is successful (200-299)\n1231\t//\t}\n1232\t//\n1233\t//\t// Accept any client error (4xx)\n1234\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range4xx) {\n1235\t//\t    // Response is a client error (400-499)\n1236\t//\t}\n1237\t//\n1238\t//\t// Custom range for specific validation\n1239\t//\tcustomRange := StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 204, Description: \"Standard success\"}\n1240\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, customRange) {\n1241\t//\t    // Response is in custom range\n1242\t//\t}\n1243\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) bool {\n1244\t\tif resp == nil {\n1245\t\t\treturn false\n1246\t\t}\n1247\t\n1248\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1249\t\t\treturn false\n1250\t\t}\n1251\t\n1252\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1253\t}\n1254\t\n1255\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails provides detailed status code range validation.\n1256\t// Like ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody interface{}, pattern EnhancedErrorMessagePattern) ErrorMessageValidationResult {\n1382\t\tresult := ErrorMessageValidationResult{\n1383\t\t\tValid:                 false,\n1384\t\t\tFound:                 false,\n1385\t\t\tMessage:               \"\",\n1386\t\t\tFieldName:             \"\",\n1387\t\t\tPatternMatched:        false,\n1388\t\t\tMustContainResults:    make(map[string]bool),\n1389\t\t\tMustNotContainResults: make(map[string]bool),\n1390\t\t\tLengthValidation:      true,\n1391\t\t\tIssues:                []string{},\n1392\t\t}\n1393\t\n1394\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1395\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is nil\")\n1396\t\t\treturn result\n1397\t\t}\n1398\t\n1399\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n1400\t\tfieldNames := pattern.FieldNames\n1401\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n1402\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n1403\t\t}\n1404\t\n1405\t\t// result\n1410\t\t}\n1411\t\n1412\t\t// Search for error message in specified fields\n1413\t\tvar foundMessage string\n1414\t\tvar foundFieldName string\n1415\t\n1416\t\t// Check primary
