Validate length\n1446\t\tif pattern.MinLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) < pattern.MinLength {\n1447\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1448\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d is less than minimum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MinLength))\n1449\t\t}\n1450\t\n1451\t\tif pattern.MaxLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) > pattern.MaxLength {\n1452\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1453\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d exceeds maximum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MaxLength))\n1454\t\t}\n1455\t\n1456\t\t// Validate regex pattern\n1457\t\tif pattern.Pattern != \"\" {\n1458\t\t\tregex := regexp.MustCompile(pattern.Pattern)\n1459\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1460\t\t\t\tregex = regexp.MustCompile(\"(?i)\" + pattern.Pattern)\n1461\t\t\t}\n1462\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = regex.MatchString(foundMessage)\n1463\t\t\tif !result.PatternMatched {\n1464\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not match required pattern: %s\", pattern.Pattern))\n1465\t\t\t}\n1466\t\t} else {\n1467\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\n1468\t\t}\n1469\t\n1470\t\t// Validate MustContain strings\n1471\t\tallMustContainFound := true\n1472\t\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\n1473\t\t\tvar found bool\n1474\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1475\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1476\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\n1477\t\t\t} else {\n1478\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\n1479\t\t\t}\n1480\t\t\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\n1481\t\t\tif !found {\n1482\t\t\t\tallMustContainFound = false\n1483\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not contain required string: %s\", mustContain))\n1484\t\t\t}\n1485\t\t}\n1486\t\n1487\t\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\n1488\t\thasForbiddenStrings := false\n1489\t\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\n1490\t\t\tvar found bool\n1491\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1492\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1493\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\n1494\t\t\t} else {\n1495\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\n1496\t\t\t}\n1497\t\t\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\n1498\t\t\tif found {\n1499\t\t\t\thasForbiddenStrings = true\n1500\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message contains forbidden string: %s\", mustNotContain))\n1501\t\t\t}\n1502\t\t}\n1503\t\n1504\t\t// Overall validity\n1505\t\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\n1506\t\t\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\n1507\t\n1508\t\treturn result\n1509\t}\n1510\t\n1511\t// =============================================================================\n1512\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\n1513\t// =============================================================================\n1514\t\n1515\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\n1516\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\n1517\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error code was found\n1518\t\tFieldName string\n1519\t\t// CodeValue is the actual error code value (as a string for flexibility)\n1520\t\tCodeValue string\n1521\t\t// NumericCode is the error code parsed as an integer (if applicable)\n1522\t\tNumericCode *int\n1523\t\t// MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched this code\n1524\t\tMatchedPattern string\n1525\t\t// Position describes where in the response the code was found\n1526\t\tPosition string\n1527\t}\n1528\t\n1529\t// EnhancedErrorCodePattern defines a pattern for finding error codes in responses.\n1530\ttype EnhancedErrorCodePattern struct {\n1531\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check for error codes\n1532\t\tFieldNames []string\n1533\t\t// NumericOnly indicates whether to only match numeric error codes\n1534\t\tNumericOnly bool\n1535\t\t// ValuePatterns contains regex patterns for valid error code values\n1536\t\tValuePatterns []string\n1537\t}\n1538\t\n1539\t// FindErrorCodesInResponse searches for error codes in an HTTP response body.\n1540\t// This is useful for validating that specific error codes are present in error responses.\n1541\t//\n1542\t// Parameters:\n1543\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n1544\t//   - patterns: The patterns to use for finding error codes\n1545\t//\n1546\t// Returns a slice of ErrorCodeMatch representing all found error codes.\n1547\t//\n1548\t// Example usage:\n1549\t//\n1550\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1551\t//\tpatterns := []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n1552\t//\t    {\n1553\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\"},\n1554\t//\t        NumericOnly: true,\n1555\t//\t    },\n1556\t//\t    {\n1557\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error\"},\n1558\t//\t        ValuePatterns: []string{\"invalid_token\", \"access_denied\"},\n1559\t//\t    },\n1560\t//\t}\n1561\t//\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(body, patterns)\n1562\t//\tfor _, match := range matches {\n1563\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Found error code: %s in field: %s\", match.CodeValue, match.FieldName)\n1564\t//\t}\n1565\tfunc FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody interface{}, patterns []EnhancedErrorCodePattern) []ErrorCodeMatch {\n1566\t\tvar matches []ErrorCodeMatch\n1567\t\n1568\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1569\t\t\treturn matches\n1570\t\t}\n1571\t\n1572\t\t// Try to convert to map\n1573\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n1574\t\tif !ok {\n1575\t\t\treturn matches\n1576\t\t}\n1577\t\n1578\t\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\n1579\t\t\t// Default to common error code fields if none specified\n1580\t\t\tfieldsToCheck := pattern.FieldNames\n1581\t\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n1582\t\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n1583\t\t\t}\n1584\t\n1585\t\t\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\n1586\t\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\n1587\t\t\t\t\tvar codeValue string\n1588\t\t\t\t\tvar numericCode *int\n1589\t\t\t\t\tvar matchedPattern string\n1590\t\n1591\t\t\t\t\t// Handle different value types\n1592\t\t\t\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n1593\t\t\t\t\tcase string:\n1594\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = v\n1595\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"string\"\n1596\t\t\t\t\t\t// Try to parse as number\n1597\t\t\t\t\t\tif num, err := parseIntFromString(v); err == nil && pattern.NumericOnly {\n1598\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n1599\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1600\t\t\t\t\tcase int:\n1601\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n1602\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &v\n1603\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n1604\t\t\t\t\tcase float64:\n1605\t\t\t\t\t\t// JSON numbers are often parsed as float64\n1606\t\t\t\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n1607\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnum := int(v)\n1608\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", num)\n1609\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n1610\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n1611\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1612\t\t\t\t\tcase bool:\n1613\t\t\t\t\t\t// Some APIs use true/false as error codes\n1614\t\t\t\t\t\tif v {\n1615\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"true\"\n1616\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n1617\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n1618\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"false\"\n1619\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n1620\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1621\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n1622\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\n1623\t\t\t\t\t}\n1624\t\n1625\t\t\t\t\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\n1626\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched := true\n1627\t\t\t\t\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\n1628\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n1629\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, valuePattern := range pattern.ValuePatterns {\n1630\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched, _ := regexp.MatchString(valuePattern, codeValue)\n1631\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif matched {\n1632\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = true\n1633\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\n1634\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1635\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1636\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1637\t\t\t\t\t}\n1638\t\n1639\t\t\t\t\t// Check numeric-only constraint\n1640\t\t\t\t\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\n1641\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n1642\t\t\t\t\t}\n1643\t\n1644\t\t\t\t\tif patternsMatched && codeValue != \"\" {\n1645\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches = append(matches, ErrorCodeMatch{\n1646\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldName:      fieldName,\n1647\t\t\t\t\t\t\tCodeValue:      codeValue,\n1648\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\n1649\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\n1650\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s' in response body\", fieldName),\n1651\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n1652\t\t\t\t\t}\n1653\t\t\t\t}\n1654\t\t\t}\n1655\t\t}\n1656\t\n1657\t\treturn matches\n1658\t}\n1659\t\n1660\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\n1661\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse for checking a single code.\n1662\t//\n1663\t// Parameters:\n1664\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body\n1665\t//   - expectedCode: The error code value to look for (as string)\n1666\t//   - fieldName: The field name to check (empty string to check all common fields)\n1667\t//\n1668\t// Returns true if the expected error code is found, false otherwise.\n1669\t//\n1670\t// Example usage:\n1671\t//\n1672\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1673\t//\thasInvalidToken := ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\", \"error\")\n1674\t//\tif hasInvalidToken {\n1675\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Response contains invalid_token error\")\n1676\t//\t}\n1677\tfunc ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\n1678\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1679\t\t\treturn false\n1680\t\t}\n1681\t\n1682\t\tvar pattern EnhancedErrorCodePattern\n1683\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1684\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n1685\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{fieldName},\n1686\t\t\t}\n1687\t\t} else {\n1688\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n1689\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"},\n1690\t\t\t}\n1691\t\t}\n1692\t\n1693\t\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody, []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{pattern})\n1694\t\tfor _, match := range matches {\n1695\t\t\tif match.CodeValue == expectedCode {\n1696\t\t\t\treturn true\n1697\t\t\t}\n1698\t\t}\n1699\t\n1700\t\treturn false\n1701\t}\n1702\t\n1703\t// parseIntFromString attempts to parse a string as an integer.\n1704\tfunc parseIntFromString(s string) (int, error) {\n1705\t\tvar result int\n1706\t\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \"%d\", &result)\n1707\t\treturn result, err\n1708\t}\n1709\t\n1710\t// =============================================================================\n1711\t// STATUS CODE RANGE VALIDATION (PATTERN-BASED)\n1712\t// =============================================================================\n1713\t\n1714\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt validates a status code against a pattern string.\n1715\t// It supports patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx' to check if a status code\n1716\t// falls within a specified range.\n1717\t//\n1718\t// Parameters:\n1719\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n1720\t//   - actual: The actual status code to validate\n1721\t//\n1722\t// Returns error if the pattern is invalid or if the actual code doesn't match the range.\n1723\t// Returns nil if the actual code falls within the specified range.\n1724\t//\n1725\t// Example usage:\n1726\t//\n1727\t//\t// Validate 404 is in 4xx range\n1728\t//\terr := ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 404)\n1729\t//\tif err != nil {\n1730\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %v\", err)\n1731\t//\t}\n1732\t//\n1733\t//\t// Validate 500 is in 5xx range\n1734\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"5xx\", 500)\n1735\t//\n1736\t//\t// This returns an error because 200 is not in 4xx range\n1737\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 200)\n1738\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(pattern string, actual int) error {\n1739\t\t// Validate pattern format\n1740\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n1741\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1742\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1743\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"pattern in format 'Nxx' (3 chars)\"),\n1744\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s' (%d chars)\", pattern, len(pattern)),\n1745\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern),\n1746\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1747\t\t\t)\n1748\t\t}\n1749\t\n1750\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n1751\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n1752\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n1753\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1754\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1755\t\t\t\t\"century digit 1-5\",\n1756\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%c' (ASCII %d)\", centuryChar, centuryChar),\n1757\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern century in '%s'\", pattern),\n1758\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1759\t\t\t)\n1760\t\t}\n1761\t\n1762\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n1763\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n1764\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1765\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1766\t\t\t\t\"pattern ending with 'xx'\",\n1767\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", pattern[1:]),\n1768\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern suffix in '%s'\", pattern),\n1769\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1770\t\t\t)\n1771\t\t}\n1772\t\n1773\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n1774\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n1775\t\tminRange := century * 100\n1776\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n1777\t\n1778\t\t// Get description for the range\n1779\t\tdesc, _ := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1780\t\n1781\t\t// Check if actual code is within the range\n1782\t\tif actual < minRange || actual > maxRange {\n1783\t\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d-%d)\", pattern, minRange, maxRange)\n1784\t\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n1785\t\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s (%d-%d)\", pattern, desc, minRange, maxRange)\n1786\t\t\t}\n1787\t\n1788\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1789\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1790\t\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1791\t\t\t\tactual,\n1792\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"status code validation failed for pattern '%s'\", pattern),\n1793\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1794\t\t\t)\n1795\t\t}\n1796\t\n1797\t\treturn nil\n1798\t}\n1799\t\n1800\t// ParseStatusCodeRange extracts the century digit and range from a pattern string.\n1801\t// This is a helper function for advanced use cases where you need the range bounds.\n1802\t//\n1803\t// Parameters:\n1804\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n1805\t//\n1806\t// Returns the min and max values of the range, or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n1807\t//\n1808\t// Example usage:\n1809\t//\n1810\t//\tmin, max, err := ParseStatusCodeRange(\"4xx\")\n1811\t//\tif err != nil {\n1812\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n1813\t//\t} else {\n1814\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Range: %d-%d\\n\", min, max)\n1815\t//\t}\n1816\tfunc ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern string) (min int, max int, err error) {\n1817\t\t// Validate pattern format\n1818\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n1819\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s (expected format: '4xx', '5xx', etc.)\", pattern)\n1820\t\t}\n1821\t\n1822\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n1823\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n1824\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n1825\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c (must be 1-5)\", centuryChar)\n1826\t\t}\n1827\t\n1828\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n1829\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n1830\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s (expected 'xx')\", pattern)\n1831\t\t}\n1832\t\n1833\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n1834\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n1835\t\tminRange := century * 100\n1836\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n1837\t\n1838\t\treturn minRange, maxRange, nil\n1839\t}\n1840\t\n1841\t// GetStatusCodeRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range pattern.\n1842\t//\n1843\t// Parameters:\n1844\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n1845\t//\n1846\t// Returns a description string or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n1847\t//\n1848\t// Example usage:\n1849\t//\n1850\t//\tdesc, err := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(\"4xx\")\n1851\t//\tif err != nil {\n1852\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n1853\t//\t} else {\n1854\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Description: %s\\n\", desc)\n1855\t//\t}\n1856\tfunc GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern string) (string, error) {\n1857\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n1858\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern)\n1859\t\t}\n1860\t\n1861\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n1862\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n1863\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c\", centuryChar)\n1864\t\t}\n1865\t\n1866\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n1867\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s\", pattern)\n1868\t\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t\tdescriptions := map[byte]string{\n1871\t\t\t'1': \"Informational (1xx)\",\n1872\t\t\t'2': \"Success (2xx)\",\n1873\t\t\t'3': \"Redirection (3xx)\",\n1874\t\t\t'4': FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING VALIDATION ERRORS\n1888\t// =============================================================================\n1889\t\n1890\t// FormatValidationError creates a standardized ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n1891\t//\n1892\t// This helper function automatically generates relevant suggestions based on the\n1893\t// validation type and the mismatch between expected and actual values.\n1894\t//\n1895\t// Parameters:\n1896\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n1897\t//   - expected: The expected value\n1898\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n1899\t//   - context: Additional context (optional)\n1900\t//   - responseSnippet: Excerpt from response (optional)\n1901\t//   - customSuggestions: Optional custom suggestions (if provided, these override auto-generated suggestions)\n1902\t//\n1903\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n1904\t//\n1905\t// suggestions:\n1910\t//\n1911\t//\terr := FormatValidationError(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", \"\",\n1912\t//\t    \"Check the endpoint documentation\", \"Verify the API version\")\n1913\tfunc FormatValidationError(validationType string, expected, actual interface{}, context, responseSnippet string, customSuggestions ...string) ValidationError {\n1914\t\tsuggestions := customSuggestions\n1915\t\tif len(customSuggestions) == 0 {\n1916\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(validationType, expected, actual)\n1917\t\t}\n1918\t\n1919\t\tve := *ValidationFormatter {\\n36\\t\\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\\n37\\t\\t\\tvalidationType: validationType,\\n38\\t\\t}\\n39\\t}\\n40\\t\\n41\\t// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\\n42\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\\n43\\t\\tvf.expected = expected\\n44\\t\\treturn vf\\n45\\t}\\n46\\t\\n47\\t// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\\n48\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\\n49\\t\\tvf.actual = actual\\n50\\t\\treturn vf\\n51\\t}\\n52\\t\\n53\\t// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\\n54\\t// Context can include information like the endpoint being tested, operation type, etc.\\n55\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n56\\t\\tvf.context = context\\n57\\t\\treturn vf\\n58\\t}\\n59\\t\\n60\\t// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\\n61\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n62\\t\\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\\n63\\t\\treturn vf\\n64\\t}\\n65\\t\\n66\\t// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\\n67\\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation.\\n68\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n69\\t\\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\\n70\\t\\treturn vf\\n71\\t}\\n72\\t\\n73\\t// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\\n74\\t// Use this when validating error messages against regex patterns.\\n75\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n76\\t\\tvf.patternDetails = details\\n77\\t\\treturn vf\\n78\\t}\\n79\\t\\n80\\t// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\\n81\\t// Use this when validating against status code ranges (e.g., \\\"4xx\\\", \\\"5xx\\\").\\n82\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n83\\t\\tvf.rangeInfo = info\\n84\\t\\treturn vf\\n85\\t}\\n86\\t\\n87\\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\\n88\\t// Use this to provide granular information about what was checked and what failed.\\n89\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n90\\t\\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\\n91\\t\\treturn vf\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\\n95\\t// If not set, suggestions will be auto-generated based on validation type and values.\\n96\\t// Use this to override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance.\\n97\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\\n98\\t\\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\\n99\\t\\treturn vf\\n100\\t}\\n101\\t\\n102\\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\\n103\\t// Returns a ValidationError that implements the error interface.\\n104\\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\\n105\\t\\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\\n106\\t\\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\\n107\\t\\t\\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\\n108\\t\\t\\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\\n109\\t\\t}\\n110\\t\\n111\\t\\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\\n112\\t\\treturn ValidationError{\\n113\\t\\t\\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\\n114\\t\\t\\tExpected:          vf.expected,\\n115\\t\\t\\tActual:            vf.actual,\\n116\\t\\t\\tContext:           vf.context,\\n117\\t\\t\\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\\n118\\t\\t\\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\\n119\\t\\t\\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\\n120\\t\\t\\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\\n121\\t\\t\\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\\n122\\t\\t\\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\\n123\\t\\t}\\n124\\t}\\n125\\t\\n126\\t// =============================================================================\\n127\\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\\n128\\t// =============================================================================\\n129\\t\\n130\\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\\n131\\t//\\n132\\t// Parameters:\\n133\\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\\n134\\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\\n135\\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \\\"GET /api/users\\\")\\n136\\t//\\n137\\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\\n138\\t//\\n139\\t// Example usage:\\n140\\t//\\n141\\t//\\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \\\"GET /api/users/123\\\")\\n142\\t//\\t// Output:\\n143\\t//\\t// status_code validation failed\\n144\\t//\\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\\n145\\t//\\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\\n146\\t//\\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\\n147\\t//\\t//   Suggestions:\\n148\\t//\\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\\n149\\t//\\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\\n150\\t//\\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\\n151\\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\\n152\\t\\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\\\"status_code\\\").\\n153\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expected).\\n154\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual).\\n155\\t\\t\\tWithContext(context).\\n156\\t\\t\\tFormat()\\n157\\t}\\n158\\t\\n159\\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\\n160\\t//\\n161\\t// Parameters:\\n162\\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \\\"invalid.*token\\\", \\\"not found\\\")\\n163\\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\\n164\\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\")\\n165\\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\\n166\\t//\\n167\\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\\n168\\t//\\n169\\t// Example usage:\\n170\\t//\\n171\\t//\\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\\\"invalid.*token\\\", \\\"access_denied\\\", \\\"error\\\", \\\"OAuth validation\\\")\\n172\\t//\\t// Output:\\n173\\t//\\t// error_message validation failed\\n174\\t//\\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\\n175\\t//\\t//   Actual:   access_denied\\n176\\t//\\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\\n177\\t//\\t//   Field:    error\\n178\\t//\\t//   Suggestions:\\n179\\t//\\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\\n180\\t//\\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\\n181\\t//\\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\\n182\\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\\n183\\t\\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\\\"error_message\\\").\\n184\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\\n185\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actualMessage).\\n186\\t\\t\\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\\n187\\t\\t\\tWithContext(context).\\n188\\t\\t\\tFormat()\\n189\\t}\\n190\\t\\n191\\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\\n192\\t//\\n193\\t// Parameters:\\n194\\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \\\"4xx\\\", \\\"5xx\\\", \\\"2xx\\\")\\n195\\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\\n196\\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\\n197\\t//\\n198\\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\\n199\\t//\\n200\\t// Example usage:\\n201\\t//\\n202\\t//\\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\\\"4xx\\\", 200, \\\"error response check\\\")\\n203\\t//\\t// Output:\\n204\\t//\\t// status_code_range validation failed\\n205\\t//\\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\\n206\\t//\\t//   Actual:   200\\n207\\t//\\t//   Context:  error response check\\n208\\t//\\t//   Suggestions:\\n209\\t//\\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\\n210\\t//\\t//     - Check authentication credentials\\n211\\t//\\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\\n212\\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\\n213\\t\\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\\n214\\t\\n215\\t\\t// Build range info string\\n216\\t\\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d-%d\\\", min, max)\\n217\\t\\tif desc != \\\"\\\" {\\n218\\t\\t\\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d-%d (%s)\\\", min, max, desc)\\n219\\t\\t}\\n220\\t\\n221\\t\\tdetails := []string{}\\n222\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n223\\t\\t\\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Pattern error: %v\\\", err))\\n224\\t\\t} else {\\n225\\t\\t\\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\\\", actual, min, max))\\n226\\t\\t}\\n227\\t\\n228\\t\\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\\\"status_code_range\\\").\\n229\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s (%s)\\\", pattern, desc)).\\n230\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual).\\n231\\t\\t\\tWithContext(context).\\n232\\t\\t\\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\\n233\\t\\n234\\t\\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\\n235\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n236\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\\n237\\t\\t}\\n238\\t\\n239\\t\\treturn formatter.Format()\\n240\\t}\\n241\\t\\n242\\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\\n243\\t//\\n244\\t// Parameters:\\n245\\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \\\"application/json\\\")\\n246\\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\\n247\\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\\n248\\t//\\n249\\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\\n250\\t//\\n251\\t// Example usage:\\n252\\t//\\n253\\t//\\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\\\"application/json\\\", \\\"text/html\\\", \\\"API response\\\")\\n254\\t//\\t// Output:\\n255\\t//\\t// content_type validation failed\\n256\\t//\\t//   Expected: application/json\\n257\\t//\\t//   Actual:   text/html\\n258\\t//   Context:  API response\\n259\\t//\\t//   Suggestions:\\n260\\t//\\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\\n261\\t//\\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\\n262\\t//\\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\\n263\\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\\n264\\t\\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\\\"content_type\\\").\\n265\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expected).\\n266\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual).\\n267\\t\\t\\tWithContext(context).\\n268\\t\\t\\tFormat()\\n269\\t}\\n270\\t\\n271\\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\\n272\\t//\\n273\\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\\n274\\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\\n275\\t// your specific requirements.\\n276\\t//\\n277\\t// Parameters:\\n278\\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\\n279\\t//   - expected: The expected value\\n280\\t//   - actual: The actual value received\\n281\\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\\n282\\t//\\n283\\t// Example usage:\\n284\\t//\\n285\\t//\\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\\n286\\t//\\t    \\\"custom_field\\\",\\n287\\t//\\t    \\\"required_value\\\",\\n288\\t//\\t    \\\"actual_value\\\",\\n289\\t//\\t    validate.WithContext(\\\"custom validation\\\"),\\n290\\t//\\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\\\"field\\\": \\\"actual_value\\\"}`),\\n291\\t//\\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\\\"Check field value\\\", \\\"Verify configuration\\\"),\\n292\\t//\\t)\\n293\\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\\n294\\t\\tvalidationType string,\\n295\\t\\texpected, actual interface{},\\n296\\t\\toptions ...FormatOption,\\n297\\t) ValidationError {\\n298\\t\\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\\n299\\t\\tfor _, opt := range options {\\n300\\t\\t\\topt(config)\\n301\\t\\t}\\n302\\t\\n303\\t\\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\\n304\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expected).\\n305\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual)\\n306\\t\\n307\\t\\tif config.Context != \\\"\\\" {\\n308\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\\n309\\t\\t}\\n310\\t\\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \\\"\\\" {\\n311\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\\n312\\t\\t}\\n313\\t\\tif config.FieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n314\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\\n315\\t\\t}\\n316\\t\\tif config.PatternDetails != \\\"\\\" {\\n317\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\\n318\\t\\t}\\n319\\t\\tif config.RangeInfo != \\\"\\\" {\\n320\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\\n321\\t\\t}\\n322\\t\\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\\n323\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\\n324\\t\\t}\\n325\\t\\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\\n326\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\\n327\\t\\t}\\n328\\t\\n329\\t\\treturn formatter.Format()\\n330\\t}\\n331\\t\\n332\\t// =============================================================================\\n333\\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\\n334\\t// =============================================================================\\n335\\t\\n336\\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\\n337\\ttype FormatConfig struct {\\n338\\t\\tContext            string\\n339\\t\\tResponseSnippet    string\\n340\\t\\tFieldName          string\\n341\\t\\tPatternDetails     string\\n342\\t\\tRangeInfo          string\\n343\\t\\tValidationDetails  []string\\n344\\t\\tSuggestions        []string\\n345\\t}\\n346\\t\\n347\\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\\n348\\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\\n349\\t\\n350\\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\\n351\\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\\n352\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n353\\t\\t\\tc.Context = context\\n354\\t\\t}\\n355\\t}\\n356\\t\\n357\\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\\n358\\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\\n359\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n360\\t\\t\\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\\n361\\t\\t}\\n362\\t}\\n363\\t\\n364\\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\\n365\\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\\n366\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n367\\t\\t\\tc.FieldName = fieldName\\n368\\t\\t}\\n369\\t}\\n370\\t\\n371\\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\\n372\\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\\n373\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n374\\t\\t\\tc.PatternDetails = details\\n375\\t\\t}\\n376\\t}\\n377\\t\\n378\\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\\n379\\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\\n380\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n381\\t\\t\\tc.RangeInfo = info\\n382\\t\\t}\\n383\\t}\\n384\\t\\n385\\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\\n386\\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\\n387\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n388\\t\\t\\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\\n389\\t\\t}\\n390\\t}\\n391\\t\\n392\\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\\n393\\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\\n394\\t\\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\\n395\\t\\t\\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\\n396\\t\\t}\\n397\\t}\\n398\\t\\n399\\t// =============================================================================\\n400\\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\\n401\\t// =============================================================================\\n402\\t\\n403\\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\\n404\\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\\n405\\t\\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\\n406\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n407\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, \\\"\\\", err\\n408\\t\\t}\\n409\\t\\n410\\t\\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\\n411\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n412\\t\\t\\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\\n413\\t\\t\\tdesc = \\\"\\\"\\n414\\t\\t}\\n415\\t\\n416\\t\\treturn min, max, desc, nil\\n417\\t}\\n418\\t\\n419\\t// =============================================================================\\n420\\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\\n421\\t// =============================================================================\\n422\\t\\n423\\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\\n424\\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\\n425\\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\\n426\\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\\n427\\t//\\n428\\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\\n429\\t// and ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification. The ErrorType enum\\n430\\t// provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\\n431\\t//\\n432\\t// Parameters:\\n433\\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \\\"status_code\\\", \\\"error_message\\\")\\n434\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n435\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n436\\t//\\n437\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n438\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n439\\t//\\n440\\t// Example usage:\\n441\\t//\\n442\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"status_code\\\", \\\"Expected 200 but got 404\\\", \\\"response\\\")\\n443\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[status_code] response: Expected 200 but got 404\\\"\\n444\\t//\\n445\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"required\\\", \\\"This field is required\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n446\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] This field is required\\\"\\n447\\t//\\n448\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"\\\", \\\"Something went wrong\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n449\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[error] Something went wrong\\\"\\n450\\t//\\n451\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"validation\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n452\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[validation] email: email validation failed\\\"\\n453\\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\\n454\\t\\t// Convert string errorType to ErrorType enum for consistent classification\\n455\\t\\t_ = ErrorTypeFromString(errorType) // Verify error type is valid\\n456\\t\\n457\\t\\t// Extract field name from variadic args\\n458\\t\\tfieldNameStr := \\\"\\\"\\n459\\t\\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\\n460\\t\\t\\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\\n461\\t\\t}\\n462\\t\\n463\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n464\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n465\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n466\\t\\t\\tif fieldNameStr != \\\"\\\" {\\n467\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldNameStr)\\n468\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n469\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n470\\t\\t\\t}\\n471\\t\\t}\\n472\\t\\n473\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage which is already integrated with ErrorType\\n474\\t\\t// This ensures consistent error classification across all errors\\n475\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\\n476\\t}\\n477\\t\\n478\\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\\n479\\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\\n480\\t// instead of string-based error types.\\n481\\t//\\n482\\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\\n483\\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\\n484\\t//\\n485\\t// Parameters:\\n486\\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\\n487\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n488\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n489\\t//\\n490\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n491\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n492\\t//\\n493\\t// Example usage:\\n494\\t//\\n495\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \\\"Field is required\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n496\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] email: Field is required\\\"\\n497\\t//\\n498\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \\\"Invalid email format\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n499\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[format] Invalid email format\\\"\\n500\\t//\\n501\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \\\"Value out of range\\\", \\\"age\\\")\\n502\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[range] age: Value out of range\\\"\\n503\\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\\n504\\t\\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\\n505\\t\\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\\n506\\t\\n507\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n508\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n509\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n510\\t\\t\\tif fieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n511\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldName)\\n512\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n513\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n514\\t\\t\\t}\\n515\\t\\t}\\n516\\t\\n517\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\\n518\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\\n519\\t}\\n520\\t\\n521\\t// =============================================================================\\n522\\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\\n523\\t// =============================================================================\\n524\\t\\n525\\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\\n526\\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\\n527\\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\\n528\\t//\\n529\\t// Parameters:\\n530\\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \\\"user.email\\\", \\\"users.0.email\\\", \\\"data.items[5]\\\")\\n531\\t//\\n532\\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\\n533\\t// Empty or invalid paths return \\\"(unknown field)\\\" for consistent error display.\\n534\\t//\\n535\\t// Example usage:\\n536\\t//\\n537\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"user.email\\\")\\n538\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"user.email\\\"\\n539\\t//\\n540\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"users.0.email\\\")\\n541\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"users[0].email\\\"\\n542\\t//\\n543\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"data.items[5]\\\")\\n544\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"data.items[5]\\\"\\n545\\t//\\n546\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"\\\")\\n547\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n548\\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\\n549\\t\\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\\n550\\t\\n551\\t\\t// Handle empty path\\n552\\t\\tif fieldPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n553\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n554\\t\\t}\\n555\\t\\n556\\t\\t// Parse and normalize the field path\\n557\\t\\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\\n558\\t}\\n559\\t\\n560\\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\\n561\\t// It converts \\\"users.0.email\\\" to \\\"users[0].email\\\" format for consistency.\\n562\\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\\n563\\t\\tif path == \\\"\\\" {\\n564\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n565\\t\\t}\\n566\\t\\n567\\t\\tparts := strings.Split(path, \\\".\\\")\\n568\\t\\tvar result []string\\n569\\t\\n570\\t\\tfor _, part := range parts {\\n571\\t\\t\\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\\n572\\t\\t\\tif part == \\\"\\\" {\\n573\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n574\\t\\t\\t}\\n575\\t\\n576\\t\\t\\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\\n577\\t\\t\\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\\n578\\t\\t\\t\\t// Convert \\\".0\\\" to \\\"[0]\\\" format\\n579\\t\\t\\t\\tif len(result) > 0 {\\n580\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Append to previous part as array index\\n581\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\\n582\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s[%s]\\\", result[lastIdx], part)\\n583\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n584\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// name to handle array indices (e.g., \\\"users.0.email\\\" → \\\"users[0].email\\\")\\n61\\t\\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\\n62\\t\\n63\\t\\t// Check if normalization resulted in field)\\\"\\n69\\t\\t}\\n70\\t\\n71\\t\\tif parent != \\\"\\\" {\\n72\\t\\t\\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\\n73\\t\\t\\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\\n74\\t\\n75\\t\\t\\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\\n76\\t\\t\\tif normalizedParent == \\\"(unknown field)\\\" {\\n77\\t\\t\\t\\treturn normalizedFieldName\\n78\\t\\t\\t}\\n79\\t\\n80\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s.%s\\\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\\n81\\t\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\t\\treturn normalizedFieldName\\n84\\t}\\n85\\t\\n86\\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\\n87\\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\\n88\\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\\n89\\t//\\n90\\t// Parameters:\\n91\\t//   - fieldName: The field name\\n92\\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \\\"line 5\\\", \\\"position 123\\\")\\n93\\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\\n94\\t//\\n95\\t// Returns a formatted location string.\\n96\\t//\\n97\\t// Example usage:\\n98\\t//\\n99\\t//\\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\\\"email\\\", \\\"line 5\\\", \\\"request\\\")\\n100\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"request.email at line 5\\\"\\n101\\t//\\n102\\t//\\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\\\"email\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"request\\\")\\n103\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"request.email\\\"\\n104\\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\\n105\\t\\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\\n106\\t\\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\\n107\\t\\n108\\t\\tif location != \\\"\\\" {\\n109\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s at %s\\\", ref, location)\\n110\\t\\t}\\n111\\t\\n112\\t\\treturn ref\\n113\\t}\\n114\\t\\n115\\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\\n116\\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\\n117\\t//\\n118\\t// Parameters:\\n119\\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\\n120\\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \\\"and\\\", \\\"or\\\")\\n121\\t//\\n122\\t// Returns a formatted list string.\\n123\\t//\\n124\\t// Example usage:\\n125\\t//\\n126\\t//\\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\\\"email\\\", \\\"password\\\"}, \\\"and\\\")\\n127\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"email and password\\\"\\n128\\t//\\n129\\t//\\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\\\"email\\\", \\\"password\\\", \\\"name\\\"}, \\\"and\\\")\\n130\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"email, password, and name\\\"\\n131\\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\\n132\\t\\tif len(fields) == 0 {\\n133\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n134\\t\\t}\\n135\\t\\n136\\t\\tif len(fields) == 1 {\\n137\\t\\t\\treturn fields[0]\\n138\\t\\t}\\n139\\t\\n140\\t\\tif len(fields) == 2 {\\n141\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s %s %s\\\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\\n142\\t\\t}\\n143\\t\\n144\\t\\t// Oxford comma style\\n145\\t\\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\\n146\\t\\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\\n147\\t\\n148\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s, %s %s\\\", strings.Join(allButLast, \\\", \\\"), conjunction, last)\\n149\\t}\\n150\\t\\n151\\t// =============================================================================\\n152\\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\\n153\\t// =============================================================================\\n154\\t\\n155\\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\\n156\\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\\n157\\t//\\n158\\t// Parameters:\\n159\\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\\n160\\t//   - message: The core error message\\n161\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\\n162\\t//\\n163\\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\\n164\\t//\\n165\\t// Example usage:\\n166\\t//\\n167\\t//\\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\\\"required\\\", \\\"Field is required\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n168\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] email: Field is required\\\"\\n169\\t//\\n170\\t//\\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\\\"format\\\", \\\"Invalid email format\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n171\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[format] Invalid email format\\\"\\n172\\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\\n173\\t\\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\\n174\\t\\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\\n175\\t\\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\\n176\\t\\n177\\t\\tvar builder strings.Builder\\n178\\t\\n179\\t\\t// Add error type in brackets\\n180\\t\\tif errorType != \\\"\\\" {\\n181\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"[%s] \\\", errorType))\\n182\\t\\t}\\n183\\t\\n184\\t\\t// Add field name if present\\n185\\t\\tif fieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n186\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s: \\\", fieldName))\\n187\\t\\t}\\n188\\t\\n189\\t\\t// Add message\\n190\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\\n191\\t\\n192\\t\\treturn builder.String()\\n193\\t}\\n194\\t\\n195\\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\\n196\\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\\n197\\t//\\n198\\t// Parameters:\\n199\\t//   - fieldName: The field name\\n200\\t//   - expected: The expected value\\n201\\t//   - actual: The actual value\\n202\\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\\n203\\t//\\n204\\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\\n205\\t//\\n206\\t// Example usage:\\n207\\t//\\n208\\t//\\tmsg := 5, 3, \\\"Not enough items\\\")\\n212\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\\\"\\n213\\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\\n214\\t\\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\\n215\\t\\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\\n216\\t\\n217\\t\\tvar builder strings.Builder\\n218\\t\\n219\\t\\tif fieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n220\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s: \\\", fieldName))\\n221\\t\\t}\\n222\\t\\n223\\t\\tif customMessage != \\\"\\\" {\\n224\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s \\\", customMessage))\\n225\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"(expected %v, got %v)\\\", expected, actual))\\n226\\t\\t} else {\\n227\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"expected %v, got %v\\\", expected, actual))\\n228\\t\\t}\\n229\\t\\n230\\t\\treturn builder.String()\\n231\\t}\\n232\\t\\n233\\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\\n234\\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\\n235\\t//\\n236\\t// Parameters:\\n237\\t//   - fieldName: The field name\\n238\\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\\n239\\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\\n240\\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\\n241\\t//\\n242\\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\\n243\\t//\\n244\\t// Example usage:\\n245\\t//\\n246\\t//\\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\\\"age\\\", 0, 120, interface{}) string {\\n249\\t\\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\\n250\\t\\n251\\t\\tif fieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n252\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, minValue, maxValue)\\n258\\t}\\n259\\t\\n260\\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\\n261\\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\\\",\\n284\\t\\t\\tactualValue, pattern)\\n285\\t}\\n286\\t\\n287\\t// =============================================================================\\n288\\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\\n289\\t// =============================================================================\\n290\\t\\n291\\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType string(errorType)\\n328\\t\\t}\\n329\\t}\\n330\\t\\n331\\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\\n332\\t// This converts technical error type strings to COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\\n471\\t// =============================================================================\\n472\\t\\n473\\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\\n474\\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\\n475\\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\\n476\\t//\\n477\\t// Parameters:\\n478\\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\\n479\\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\\n480\\t//\\n481\\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\\n482\\t//\\n483\\t// Example usage:\\n484\\t//\\n485\\t//\\terr := ValidationError{\\n486\\t//\\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\\n487\\t//\\t    Message: \\\"Field is required\\\",\\n488\\t//\\t    FieldName: \\\"email\\\",\\n489\\t//\\t}\\n490\\t//\\n491\\t//\\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\\n492\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[High] [required] email: Field is required\\\"\\n493\\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\\n494\\t\\tvar builder strings.Builder\\n495\\t\\n496\\t\\t// Add severity if requested\\n497\\t\\tif includeSeverity {\\n498\\t\\t\\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\\n499\\t\\t\\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\\n500\\t\\t\\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\\n501\\t\\t\\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\\n502\\t\\t\\t\\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\\n503\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n504\\t\\t\\t\\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\\n505\\t\\t\\t\\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\\n506\\t\\t\\t}\\n507\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s \\\", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\\n508\\t\\t}\\n509\\t\\n510\\t\\t// Add error type and field\\n511\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\\n512\\t\\n513\\t\\t// Add expected/actual values if present\\n514\\t\\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\\n515\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(\\\" (\\\")\\n516\\t\\t\\tif err.Expected != nil {\\n517\\t\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"expected: %v\\\", err.Expected))\\n518\\t\\t\\t}\\n519\\t\\t\\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\\n520\\t\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(\\\", \\\")\\n521\\t\\t\\t}\\n522\\t\\t\\tif err.Actual != nil {\\n523\\t\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"actual: %v\\\", err.Actual))\\n524\\t\\t\\t}\\n525\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(\\\")\\\")\\n526\\t\\t}\\n527\\t\\n528\\t\\t// Add location if present\\n529\\t\\tif err.Location != \\\"\\\" {\\n530\\t\\t\\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\" [%s]\\\", err.Location))\\n531\\t\\t}\\n532\\t\\n533\\t\\treturn builder.String()\\n534\\t}\\n535\\t\\n536\\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\\n537\\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\\n538\\t//\\n539\\t// =============================================================================\\n565\\t\\n566\\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\\n567\\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\\n568\\t//\\n569\\t// Parameters:\\n570\\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\\n571\\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\\n572\\t//\\n573\\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\\n574\\t//\\n575\\t// Example usage:\\n576\\t//\\n577\\t//\\terrors := []ValidationError{{\\n578\\t//\\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\\n579\\t//\\t    Message: \\\"Field is required\\\",\\n580\\t//\\t    FieldName: \\\"email\\\",\\n581\\t//\\t}, {\\n582\\t//\\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\\n583\\t//\\t    Message: \\\"Invalid format\\\",\\n584\\t//\\t    FieldName: \\\"password\\\",\\n585\\t//\\t}}\\n586\\t//\\n587\\t//\\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\\n588\\t//\\t// Returns:\\n589\\t//\\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\\n590\\t//\\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\\n591\\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\\n592\\t\\tif len(errors) == 0 {\\n593\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"No errors\\\"\\n594\\t\\t}\\n595\\t\\n596\\t\\tvar lines []string\\n597\\t\\tfor i, err := range errors {\\n598\\t\\t\\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d. \\\", i+1)\\n599\\t\\t\\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\\n600\\t\\t\\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\\n601\\t\\t}\\n602\\t\\n603\\t\\treturn strings.Join(lines, \\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n604\\t}\\n605\\t\\n606\\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\\n607\\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\\n608\\t//\\n609\\t// Parameters:\\n610\\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\\n611\\t//\\n612\\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\\n613\\t//\\n614\\t// Example usage:\\n615\\t//\\n616\\t//\\terrors := []ValidationError{{\\n617\\t//\\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\\n618\\t//\\t    FieldName: \\\"email\\\",\\n619\\t//\\t}, {\\n620\\t//\\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\\n621\\t//\\t    FieldName: \\\"password\\\",\\n622\\t//\\t}}\\n623\\t//\\n624\\t//\\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\\n625\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"2 errors: required (2)\\\"\\n626\\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\\n627\\t\\tif len(errors) == 0 {\\n628\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"No errors\\\"\\n629\\t\\t}\\n630\\t\\n631\\t\\t// Count errors by type\\n632\\t\\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\\n633\\t\\tfor _, err := range errors {\\n634\\t\\t\\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\\n635\\t\\t}\\n636\\t\\n637\\t\\t// Build summary parts\\n638\\t\\tvar parts []string\\n639\\t\\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\\n640\\t\\t\\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s (%d)\\\", errorType, count))\\n641\\t\\t}\\n642\\t\\n643\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d error(s): %s\\\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \\\", \\\"))\\n644\\t}\\n645\\t\\n646\\t// =============================================================================\\n647\\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\\n648\\t//
