600\\n99\\t}\\n100\\t\\n101\\t// ContentTypeIsValid checks if a response's Content-Type header matches the expected pattern.\\n102\\t// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\\n103\\t// For example, \\\"application/json\\\" will match \\\"application/json; charset=utf-8\\\".\\n104\\t//\\n105\\t// Parameters:\\n106\\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\\n107\\t//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \\\"application/json\\\")\\n108\\t//\\n109\\t// Returns true if the response Content-Type matches the expected pattern, false otherwise.\\n110\\t// Returns false if the response is nil or has no Content-Type header.\\n111\\t//\\n112\\t// Example usage:\\n113\\t//\\n114\\t//\\tif ContentTypeIsValid(response, \\\"application/json\\\") {\\n115\\t//\\t    // Handle JSON response\\n116\\t//\\t}\\n117\\tfunc ContentTypeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected string) bool {\\n118\\t\\tif resp == nil {\\n119\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n120\\t\\t}\\n121\\t\\n122\\t\\tactual := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\")\\n123\\t\\tif actual == \\\"\\\" {\\n124\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n125\\t\\t}\\n126\\t\\n127\\t\\t// Parse both content-type strings to extract base media types\\n128\\t\\tactualMediaType := parseContentType(actual)\\n129\\t\\texpectedMediaType := parseContentType(expected)\\n130\\t\\n131\\t\\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\\n132\\t}\\n133\\t\\n134\\t// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\\n135\\t// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\\n136\\t// For example, \\\"application/json; charset=utf-8\\\" becomes \\\"application/json\\\".\\n137\\tfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\\n138\\t\\tif contentType == \\\"\\\" {\\n139\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n140\\t\\t}\\n141\\t\\n142\\t\\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\\n143\\t\\t// The first part before any semicolon is the base media type\\n144\\t\\tfor i, c := range contentType {\\n145\\t\\t\\tif c == ';' {\\n146\\t\\t\\t\\treturn trimSpace(contentType[:i])\\n147\\t\\t\\t}\\n148\\t\\t}\\n149\\t\\n150\\t\\t// No parameters found, return the whole string trimmed\\n151\\t\\treturn trimSpace(contentType)\\n152\\t}\\n153\\t\\n154\\t// trimSpace removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.\\n155\\tfunc trimSpace(s string) string {\\n156\\t\\tstart := 0\\n157\\t\\tend := len(s)\\n158\\t\\n159\\t\\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\\\t' || s[start] == '\\\\n' || s[start] == '\\\\r') {\\n160\\t\\t\\tstart++\\n161\\t\\t}\\n162\\t\\n163\\t\\tfor end > start && (s[end-1] == ' ' || s[end-1] == '\\\\t' || s[end-1] == '\\\\n' || s[end-1] == '\\\\r') {\\n164\\t\\t\\tend--\\n165\\t\\t}\\n166\\t\\n167\\t\\treturn s[start:end]\\n168\\t}\\n169\\t\\n170\\t// ErrorResponseFieldNames specifies custom field names to check when validating error response structures.\\n171\\t// If nil, the default field names \\\"error\\\" and \\\"message\\\" are checked.\\n172\\ttype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\\n173\\t\\t// PrimaryFieldName is the main error field to check (e.g., \\\"error\\\", \\\"detail\\\", \\\"error_description\\\")\\n174\\t\\tPrimaryFieldName string\\n175\\t\\t// SecondaryFieldName is an optional secondary field to check (e.g., \\\"message\\\", \\\"description\\\")\\n176\\t\\tSecondaryFieldName string\\n177\\t}\\n178\\t\\n179\\t// DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames returns the default field names used for error response validation.\\n180\\t// This checks for both \\\"error\\\" and \\\"message\\\" fields.\\n181\\tfunc DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames() *ErrorResponseFieldNames {\\n182\\t\\treturn &ErrorResponseFieldNames{\\n183\\t\\t\\tPrimaryFieldName:   \\\"error\\\",\\n184\\t\\t\\tSecondaryFieldName: \\\"message\\\",\\n185\\t\\t}\\n186\\t}\\n187\\t\\n188\\t// ErrorResponseStructureIsValid validates whether a response object contains a valid error response structure.\\n189\\t// It checks for the presence of common error fields and returns whether the structure is valid.\\n190\\t//\\n191\\t// The response parameter can be:\\n192\\t//   - map[string]interface{}: A parsed JSON object representing the response body\\n193\\t//   - Any type that can be inspected for error fields using reflection\\n194\\t//\\n195\\t// The fieldNames parameter allows customization of which fields to check:\\n196\\t//   - If nil, checks for default fields \\\"error\\\" and \\\"message\\\"\\n197\\t//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\\n198\\t//\\n199\\t// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\\n200\\t// false otherwise.\\n201\\t//\\n202\\t// Example usage:\\n203\\t//\\n204\\t//\\t// Using default field names (checks for \\\"error\\\" and \\\"message\\\")\\n205\\t//\\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Invalid input\\\"}\\n206\\t//\\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\\n207\\t//\\n208\\t//\\t// Using custom field names\\n209\\t//\\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \\\"detail\\\", SecondaryFieldName: \\\"description\\\"}\\n210\\t//\\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"Resource not found\\\"}\\n211\\t//\\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\\n212\\tfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\\n213\\t\\tif response == nil {\\n214\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n215\\t\\t}\\n216\\t\\n217\\t\\t// Use default field names if not provided\\n218\\t\\tif fieldNames == nil {\\n219\\t\\t\\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\\n220\\t\\t}\\n221\\t\\n222\\t\\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\\n223\\t\\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\\n224\\t\\tif !ok {\\n225\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n226\\t\\t}\\n227\\t\\n228\\t\\t// Check primary field\\n229\\t\\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n230\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\\n231\\t\\t\\t\\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\\n232\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n233\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n234\\t\\t\\t}\\n235\\t\\t}\\n236\\t\\n237\\t\\t// Check secondary field\\n238\\t\\tif fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n239\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\\n240\\t\\t\\t\\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\\n241\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n242\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n243\\t\\t\\t}\\n244\\t\\t}\\n245\\t\\n246\\t\\treturn false\\n247\\t}\\n248\\t\\n249\\t// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\\n250\\t// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\\n251\\t// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\\n252\\tfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\\n253\\t\\tif value == nil {\\n254\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n255\\t\\t}\\n256\\t\\n257\\t\\tswitch v := value.(type) {\\n258\\t\\tcase string:\\n259\\t\\t\\treturn v != \\\"\\\"\\n260\\t\\tcase int:\\n261\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n262\\t\\tcase int8:\\n263\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n264\\t\\tcase int16:\\n265\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n266\\t\\tcase int32:\\n267\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n268\\t\\tcase int64:\\n269\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n270\\t\\tcase uint:\\n271\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n272\\t\\tcase uint8:\\n273\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n274\\t\\tcase uint16:\\n275\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n276\\t\\tcase uint32:\\n277\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n278\\t\\tcase uint64:\\n279\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0\\n280\\t\\tcase float32:\\n281\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0.0\\n282\\t\\tcase float64:\\n283\\t\\t\\treturn v != 0.0\\n284\\t\\tcase bool:\\n285\\t\\t\\treturn v\\n286\\t\\tcase map[string]interface{}:\\n287\\t\\t\\treturn len(v) > 0\\n288\\t\\tcase []interface{}:\\n289\\t\\t\\treturn len(v) > 0\\n290\\t\\tdefault:\\n291\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n292\\t\\t}\\n293\\t}\\n294\\t\\n295\\t// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\\n296\\ttype CORSConfig struct {\\n297\\t\\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\\n298\\t\\t// Use \\\"*\\\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \\\"https://example.com\\\".\\n299\\t\\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\\n300\\t\\tAllowOrigin string\\n301\\t\\n302\\t\\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\\n303\\t\\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\\n304\\t\\tAllowMethods string\\n305\\t\\n306\\t\\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\\n307\\t\\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\\n308\\t\\tAllowHeaders string\\n309\\t\\n310\\t\\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\\n311\\t\\t// If true, expects \\\"true\\\"; if false, expects \\\"true\\\" or header not present.\\n312\\t\\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\\n313\\t\\tAllowCredentials bool\\n314\\t\\n315\\t\\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\\n316\\t\\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\\n317\\t\\tExposeHeaders string\\n318\\t\\n319\\t\\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\\n320\\t\\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\\n321\\t\\tMaxAge string\\n322\\t}\\n323\\t\\n324\\t// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\\n325\\t// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\\n326\\t//\\n327\\t// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\\n328\\t// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\\n329\\t//\\n330\\t// Parameters:\\n331\\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\\n332\\t//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\\n333\\t//\\n334\\t// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\\n335\\t// Returns false if the response is nil.\\n336\\t//\\n337\\t// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\\n338\\t// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\\n339\\t//\\n340\\t// Example usage:\\n341\\t//\\n342\\t//\\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\\n343\\t//\\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\\n344\\t//\\t    // CORS headers are present\\n345\\t//\\t}\\n346\\t//\\n347\\t//\\t// Validate specific origin\\n348\\t//\\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \\\"https://example.com\\\"}\\n349\\t//\\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\\n350\\t//\\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\\n351\\t//\\t}\\n352\\t//\\n353\\t//\\t// Validate wildcard CORS\\n354\\t//\\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \\\"*\\\", AllowCredentials: false}\\n355\\t//\\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\\n356\\t//\\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\\n357\\t//\\t}\\n358\\t//\\n359\\t//\\t// Validate full CORS configuration\\n360\\t//\\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\\n361\\t//\\t    AllowOrigin: \\\"https://example.com\\\",\\n362\\t//\\t    AllowMethods: \\\"GET, POST, OPTIONS\\\",\\n363\\t//\\t    AllowHeaders: \\\"Content-Type, Authorization\\\",\\n364\\t//\\t    AllowCredentials: true,\\n365\\t//\\t}\\n366\\t//\\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\\n367\\t//\\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\\n368\\t//\\t}\\n369\\tfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\\n370\\t\\tif resp == nil {\\n371\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n372\\t\\t}\\n373\\t\\n374\\t\\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\\n375\\t\\tif config == nil {\\n376\\t\\t\\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\\\")\\n377\\t\\t\\treturn origin != \\\"\\\"\\n378\\t\\t}\\n379\\t\\n380\\t\\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\\n381\\t\\tif config.AllowOrigin != \\\"\\\" {\\n382\\t\\t\\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\\\")\\n383\\t\\t\\tif origin == \\\"\\\" {\\n384\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n385\\t\\t\\t}\\n386\\t\\n387\\t\\t\\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\\n388\\t\\t\\tif config.AllowOrigin == \\\"*\\\" {\\n389\\t\\t\\t\\t// Wildcard is valid\\n390\\t\\t\\t\\tif origin != \\\"*\\\" {\\n391\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n392\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n393\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n394\\t\\t\\t\\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\\n395\\t\\t\\t\\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\\n396\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n397\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n398\\t\\t\\t}\\n399\\t\\t}\\n400\\t\\n401\\t\\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\\n402\\t\\tif config.AllowMethods != \\\"\\\" {\\n403\\t\\t\\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\\\")\\n404\\t\\t\\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\\n405\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n406\\t\\t\\t}\\n407\\t\\t}\\n408\\t\\n409\\t\\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\\n410\\t\\tif config.AllowHeaders != \\\"\\\" {\\n411\\t\\t\\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\\\")\\n412\\t\\t\\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\\n413\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n414\\t\\t\\t}\\n415\\t\\t}\\n416\\t\\n417\\t\\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\\n418\\t\\tif config.AllowCredentials {\\n419\\t\\t\\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\\\")\\n420\\t\\t\\tif credentials != \\\"true\\\" {\\n421\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n422\\t\\t\\t}\\n423\\t\\t}\\n424\\t\\n425\\t\\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\\n426\\t\\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \\\"\\\" {\\n427\\t\\t\\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\\\")\\n428\\t\\t\\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\\n429\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n430\\t\\t\\t}\\n431\\t\\t}\\n432\\t\\n433\\t\\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\\n434\\t\\tif config.MaxAge != \\\"\\\" {\\n435\\t\\t\\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Access-Control-Max-Age\\\")\\n436\\t\\t\\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\\n437\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n438\\t\\t\\t}\\n439\\t\\t}\\n440\\t\\n441\\t\\treturn true\\n442\\t}\\n443\\t\\n444\\t// =============================================================================\\n445\\t// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\\n446\\t// =============================================================================\\n447\\t\\n448\\t// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\\n449\\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\\n450\\t\\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\\n451\\t\\tPattern string\\n452\\t\\n453\\t\\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\\n454\\t\\tCaseInsensitive bool\\n455\\t\\n456\\t\\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\\n457\\t\\tMatchAny bool\\n458\\t\\n459\\t\\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\", \\\"detail\\\")\\n460\\t\\t// If empty, checks default fields: \\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\"\\n461\\t\\tFieldNames []string\\n462\\t}\\n463\\t\\n464\\t// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\\n465\\t//\\n466\\t// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\\n467\\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\\n468\\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\\n469\\t//\\n470\\t// Parameters:\\n471\\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\\n472\\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\\n473\\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\\n474\\t//\\n475\\t// Returns:\\n476\\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\\n477\\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\\n478\\t//\\n479\\t// Example usage:\\n480\\t//\\n481\\t//\\t// Check for \\\"not found\\\" error (case-insensitive)\\n482\\t//\\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\\n483\\t//\\n484\\t//\\t// Check for \\\"invalid.*token\\\" pattern\\n485\\t//\\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\\n486\\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\\n487\\t\\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\\n488\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"response body is empty\\\")\\n489\\t\\t}\\n490\\t\\n491\\t\\tif pattern == \\\"\\\" {\\n492\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"pattern cannot be empty\\\")\\n493\\t\\t}\\n494\\t\\n495\\t\\t// Parse JSON body\\n496\\t\\tvar body map[string]interface{}\\n497\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\\n498\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse response body: %w\\\", err)\\n499\\t\\t}\\n500\\t\\n501\\t\\t// Compile regex pattern\\n502\\t\\tregexPattern := pattern\\n503\\t\\tif caseInsensitive {\\n504\\t\\t\\tregexPattern = \\\"(?i)\\\" + pattern\\n505\\t\\t}\\n506\\t\\n507\\t\\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\\n508\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n509\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid regex pattern: %w\\\", err)\\n510\\t\\t}\\n511\\t\\n512\\t\\t// Check default error fields\\n513\\t\\tdefaultFields := []string{\\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\", \\\"detail\\\", \\\"description\\\"}\\n514\\t\\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\\n515\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\\n516\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\\n517\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\\n518\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n519\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n520\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n521\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check nested error objects\\n522\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n523\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\\\"message\\\"]; exists {\\n524\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\\n525\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\\n526\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n527\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n528\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n529\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n530\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n531\\t\\t\\t}\\n532\\t\\t}\\n533\\t\\n534\\t\\treturn false, nil\\n535\\t}\\n536\\t\\n537\\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\\n538\\t//\\n539\\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\\n540\\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\\n541\\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\\n542\\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\\n543\\t//\\n544\\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\\n545\\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\\\), it's treated as regex\\n546\\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\\n547\\t//\\n548\\t// Parameters:\\n549\\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\\n550\\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\\n551\\t//\\n552\\t// Returns:\\n553\\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\\n554\\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\\n555\\t//\\n556\\t// Example usage:\\n557\\t//\\n558\\t//\\t// Regex pattern matching\\n559\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"invalid.*token\\\")\\n560\\t//\\tif err != nil {\\n561\\t//\\t    // Pattern not found\\n562\\t//\\t}\\n563\\t//\\n564\\t//\\t// Simple substring matching\\n565\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"not found\\\")\\n566\\t//\\tif err != nil {\\n567\\t//\\t    // Substring not found\\n568\\t//\\t}\\n569\\t//\\n570\\t//\\t// Common error patterns\\n571\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"unauthorized\\\")        // OAuth errors\\n572\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"invalid.*credentials\\\") // Auth errors\\n573\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"rate.*limit\\\")          // Rate limiting\\n574\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"timeout\\\")               // Timeout errors\\n575\\t//\\n576\\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\\n577\\t//\\n578\\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\\n579\\t//\\n580\\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\\n581\\t//\\n582\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"invalid_token\\\", \\\"error_description\\\": \\\"The access token expired\\\"}`)\\n583\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"invalid_token\\\")  // substring match\\n584\\t//\\n585\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"access_denied\\\"}`)\\n586\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"access_denied|invalid_grant\\\")  // regex match\\n587\\t//\\n588\\t// # Authentication Errors\\n589\\t//\\n590\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\\\"}`)\\n591\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"authentication.*failed\\\")  // regex match\\n592\\t//\\n593\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Invalid username or password\\\"}`)\\n594\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"invalid.*credentials\\\")  // regex match\\n595\\t//\\n596\\t// # Authorization Errors\\n597\\t//\\n598\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Permission denied\\\"}`)\\n599\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"permission.*denied\\\")  // regex match\\n600\\t//\\n601\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"User is not authorized to access this resource\\\"}`)\\n602\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"not.*authorized\\\")  // regex match\\n603\\t//\\n604\\t// # Validation Errors\\n605\\t//\\n606\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Validation failed: required field missing\\\"}`)\\n607\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"validation.*failed\\\")  // regex match\\n608\\t//\\n609\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\\\"}`)\\n610\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"invalid.*input\\\")  // regex match\\n611\\t//\\n612\\t// # Resource Errors\\n613\\t//\\n614\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Resource not found\\\"}`)\\n615\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"not found\\\")  // substring match\\n616\\t//\\n617\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"User does not exist\\\"}`)\\n618\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"does not exist\\\")  // substring match\\n619\\t//\\n620\\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\\n621\\t//\\n622\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Rate limit exceeded\\\"}`)\\n623\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"rate.*limit\\\")  // regex match\\n624\\t//\\n625\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"Too many requests, please try again later\\\"}`)\\n626\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"too many requests\\\")  // substring match\\n627\\t//\\n628\\t// # Timeout Errors\\n629\\t//\\n630\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"Request {\\n650\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"expected pattern cannot be empty\\\")\\n651\\t\\t}\\n652\\t\\n653\\t\\t// Parse JSON body\\n654\\t\\tvar body map[string]interface{}\\n655\\t\\tif err := fields\\n660\\t\\tdefaultFields := []string{\\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\", \\\"detail\\\", \\\"description\\\", \\\"error_description\\\"}\\n661\\t\\tvar foundMessages != \\\"\\\" {\\n672\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, =============================================================================\\n1076\\t\\n1077\\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\\n1078\\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\\n1079\\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\\n1080\\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\\n1081\\t\\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\\n1082\\t\\tValid bool\\n1083\\t\\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\\n1084\\t\\tActualCode int\\n1085\\t\\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\\n1086\\t\\tExpectedCodes []int\\n1087\\t\\t// MatchedCode is the error (5xx)\\n1094\\t\\tIsServerError bool\\n1095\\t\\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\\n1096\\t\\tCategory string\\n1097\\t}\\n1098\\t\\n1099\\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\\n1100\\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\\n1101\\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\\n1102\\t//\\n1103\\t// Parameters:\\n1104\\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\\n1105\\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\\n1106\\t//\\n1107\\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\\n1108\\t//\\n1109\\t// Example usage:\\n1110\\t//\\n1111\\t//\\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\\n1112\\t//\\tif !result.Valid {\\n1113\\t//\\t {\\n1120\\t//\\t    log.Printf(\\\"Status code is valid: %d\\\", result.ActualCode)\\n1121\\t//\\t}\\n1122\\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) APIs use true/false as error codes\\n1614\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif v {\\n1615\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = \\\"true\\\"\\n1616\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"boolean\\\"\\n1617\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1618\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = \\\"false\\\"\\n1619\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"boolean\\\"\\n1620\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1621\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n1622\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\\n1623\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1624\\t\\n1625\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\\n1626\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched := true\\n1627\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\\n1628\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched = false\\n1629\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, valuePattern := range pattern.ValuePatterns {\\n1630\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatched, _ := regexp.MatchString(valuePattern, codeValue)\\n1631\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif matched {\\n1632\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched = true\\n1633\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\\n1634\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n1635\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1636\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1637\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1638\\t\\n1639\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check numeric-only constraint\\n1640\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\\n1641\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched = false\\n1642\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1643\\t\\n1644\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif patternsMatched codeValue,\\n1648\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\\n1649\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\\n1650\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\\\"field '%s' in matches\\n1658\\t}\\n1659\\t\\n1660\\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\\n1661\\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\\n1678\\t\\tif responseBody == nil {\\n1679\\t\\t\\treturn &result)\\n1707\\t\\treturn result, err\\n1708\\t}\\n1709\\t\\n1710\\t// =============================================================================\\n1711\\t// STATUS CODE RANGE VALIDATION (PATTERN-BASED)\\n1712\\t// =============================================================================\\n1713\\t\\n1714\\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt validates a status code against a pattern string.\\n1715\\t// It supports patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx' to check if a status code\\n1716\\t// falls within a specified range.\\n1717\\t//\\n1718\\t// Parameters:\\n1719\\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\\n1720\\t//   - actual: The actual status code to validate\\n1721\\t//\\n1722\\t// Returns error if the pattern is invalid or if the actual pattern),\\n1746\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"\\\",\\n1747\\t\\t\\t)\\n1748\\t\\t}\\n1749\\t\\n1750\\t\\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\\n1751\\t\\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\\n1752\\t\\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\\n1753\\t\\t\\treturn FormatValidationError(\\n1754\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"status_code_range\\\",\\n1755\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"century digit string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\\n1805\\t//\\n1806\\t// Returns the min and max values of the range, or an error if the pattern is invalid.\\n1807\\t//\\n1808\\t// Example error information than FormatValidationError provides.\\n1937\\t//\\n1938\\t// Parameters:\\n1939\\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \\\"status_code\\\", \\\"error_message\\\", \\\"content_type\\\")\\n1940\\t//   - expected: The expected value\\n1941\\t//   - actual: The actual value received\\n1942\\t//   - context: Additional context about the validation operation\\n1943\\t//   - responseSnippet: Excerpt from the response for debugging\\n1944\\t//   - fieldName: The specific field name where the error was found (e.g., \\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\")\\n1945\\t//   - location: Where in the input the error occurred (e.g., \\\"line 5\\\", \\\"field 'user.email'\\\", \\\"position 123\\\")\\n1946\\t//   - relatedFields: List of fields failed\\\", validationType)\\n2024\\t\\n2025\\t\\t// Add expected vs actual information if available\\n2026\\t\\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\\n2027\\t\\t\\tmsg += \\\": expected \\\"\\n2028\\t\\t\\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\\n2029\\t\\t\\tcase int:\\n2030\\t\\t\\t\\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d\\\", exp)\\n2031\\t\\t\\tcase string:\\n2032\\t\\t\\t\\tmsg += {\\n2037\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmsg += \\\", \\\"\\n2038\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2039\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d\\\", code)\\n2040\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2041\\t\\t\\t\\tmsg += {\\n2048\\t\\t\\tcase int:\\n2049\\t\\t\\t\\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d\\\", act)\\n2050\\t\\t\\tcase string:\\n2051\\t\\t\\t\\tif len(act) > 50 {\\n2052\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tact = act[:50] + This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\\n425\\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\\n426\\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\\n427\\t//\\n428\\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\\n429\\t// and ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification. The ErrorType enum\\n430\\t// provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\\n431\\t//\\n432\\t// Parameters:\\n433\\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \\\"status_code\\\", \\\"error_message\\\")\\n434\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n435\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n436\\t//\\n437\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n438\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n439\\t//\\n440\\t// Example usage:\\n441\\t//\\n442\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"status_code\\\", \\\"Expected 200 but got 404\\\", \\\"response\\\")\\n443\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[status_code] response: required\\\"\\n447\\t//\\n448\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"\\\", \\\"Something went wrong\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n449\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[error] Something went wrong\\\"\\n450\\t//\\n451\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"validation\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n452\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[validation] email: email validation failed\\\"\\n453\\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\\n454\\t\\t// Convert string errorType to ErrorType enum for consistent classification\\n455\\t\\t_ = ErrorTypeFromString(errorType) // Verify error type is valid\\n456\\t\\n457\\t\\t// Extract field name from variadic args\\n458\\t\\tfieldNameStr := \\\"\\\"\\n459\\t\\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\\n460\\t\\t\\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\\n461\\t\\t}\\n462\\t\\n463\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n464\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n465\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n466\\t\\t\\tif fieldNameStr != \\\"\\\" {\\n467\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldNameStr)\\n468\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n469\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n470\\t\\t\\t}\\n471\\t\\t}\\n472\\t\\n473\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage which is already integrated with ErrorType\\n474\\t\\t// This ensures consistent error classification across all errors\\n475\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\\n476\\t}\\n477\\t\\n478\\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\\n479\\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\\n480\\t// instead of string-based error types.\\n481\\t//\\n482\\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\\n483\\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\\n484\\t//\\n485\\t// Parameters:\\n486\\t//   - \\\"users.0.email\\\" to \\\"users[0].email\\\" format for consistency.\\n562\\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\\n563\\t\\tif path == \\\"\\\" {\\n564\\t\\t\\treturn This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\\n467\\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\\n468\\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\\n469\\t//\\n470\\t// Parameters:\\n471\\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\\n472\\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\\n473\\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\\n474\\t//\\n475\\t// Returns:\\n476\\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\\n477\\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\\n478\\t//\\n479\\t// Example usage:\\n480\\t//\\n481\\t//\\t// Check for \\\"not found\\\" error (case-insensitive)\\n482\\t//\\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\\n483\\t//\\n484\\t//\\t// Check for \\\"invalid.*token\\\" pattern\\n485\\t//\\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\\n486\\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\\n487\\t\\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\\n488\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"response body is empty\\\")\\n489\\t\\t}\\n490\\t\\n491\\t\\tif pattern == \\\"\\\" {\\n492\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"pattern cannot be empty\\\")\\n493\\t\\t}\\n494\\t\\n495\\t\\t// Parse JSON body\\n496\\t\\tvar body map[string]interface{}\\n497\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\\n498\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse response body: %w\\\", err)\\n499\\t\\t}\\n500\\t\\n501\\t\\t// Compile regex pattern\\n502\\t\\tregexPattern := pattern\\n503\\t\\tif caseInsensitive {\\n504\\t\\t\\tregexPattern = \\\"(?i)\\\" + pattern\\n505\\t\\t}\\n506\\t\\n507\\t\\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\\n508\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n509\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid regex pattern: %w\\\", err)\\n510\\t\\t}\\n511\\t\\n512\\t\\t// Check default error fields\\n513\\t\\tdefaultFields := []string{\\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\", \\\"detail\\\", \\\"description\\\"}\\n514\\t\\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\\n515\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\\n516\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\\n517\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\\n518\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n519\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n520\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n521\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check nested error objects\\n522\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n523\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\\\"message\\\"]; exists {\\n524\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\\n525\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\\n526\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n527\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n528\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n529\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n530\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n531\\t\\t\\t}\\n532\\t\\t}\\n533\\t\\n534\\t\\treturn false, nil\\n535\\t}\\n536\\t\\n537\\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\\n538\\t//\\n539\\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\\n540\\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\\n541\\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\\n542\\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\\n543\\t//\\n544\\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\\n545\\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\\\), it's treated as regex\\n546\\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\\n547\\t//\\n548\\t// Parameters:\\n549\\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\\n550\\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\\n551\\t//\\n552\\t// Returns:\\n553\\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\\n554\\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\\n555\\t//\\n556\\t// Example usage:\\n557\\t//\\n558\\t//\\t// Regex pattern matching\\n559\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"invalid.*token\\\")\\n560\\t//\\tif err != nil {\\n561\\t//\\t    // Pattern not found\\n562\\t//\\t}\\n563\\t//\\n564\\t//\\t// Simple substring matching\\n565\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"not found\\\")\\n566\\t//\\tif err != nil {\\n567\\t//\\t    // Substring not found\\n568\\t//\\t}\\n569\\t//\\n570\\t//\\t// Common error patterns\\n571\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"unauthorized\\\")        // OAuth errors\\n572\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"invalid.*credentials\\\") // Auth errors\\n573\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"rate.*limit\\\")          // Rate limiting\\n574\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \\\"timeout\\\")               // Timeout errors\\n575\\t//\\n576\\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\\n577\\t//\\n578\\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\\n579\\t//\\n580\\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\\n581\\t//\\n582\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"invalid_token\\\", \\\"error_description\\\": \\\"The access token expired\\\"}`)\\n583\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"invalid_token\\\")  // substring match\\n584\\t//\\n585\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"access_denied\\\"}`)\\n586\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"access_denied|invalid_grant\\\")  // regex match\\n587\\t//\\n588\\t// # Authentication Errors\\n589\\t//\\n590\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\\\"}`)\\n591\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"authentication.*failed\\\")  // regex match\\n592\\t//\\n593\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Invalid username or password\\\"}`)\\n594\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"invalid.*credentials\\\")  // regex match\\n595\\t//\\n596\\t// # Authorization Errors\\n597\\t//\\n598\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Permission denied\\\"}`)\\n599\\t//\\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"permission.*denied\\\")  // regex match\\n600\\t//\\n601\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"User is not authorized to access this resource\\\"}`)\\n602\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"not.*authorized\\\")  // regex match\\n603\\t//\\n604\\t// # Validation Errors\\n605\\t//\\n606\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Validation failed: required field missing\\\"}`)\\n607\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"validation.*failed\\\")  // regex match\\n608\\t//\\n609\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\\\"}`)\\n610\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"invalid.*input\\\")  // regex match\\n611\\t//\\n612\\t// # Resource Errors\\n613\\t//\\n614\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Resource not found\\\"}`)\\n615\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"not found\\\")  // substring match\\n616\\t//\\n617\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"User does not exist\\\"}`)\\n618\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"does not exist\\\")  // substring match\\n619\\t//\\n620\\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\\n621\\t//\\n622\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Rate limit exceeded\\\"}`)\\n623\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"rate.*limit\\\")  // regex match\\n624\\t//\\n625\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"detail\\\": \\\"Too many requests, please try again later\\\"}`)\\n626\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"too many requests\\\")  // substring match\\n627\\t//\\n628\\t// # Timeout Errors\\n629\\t//\\n630\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"Request timeout\\\"}`)\\n631\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"timeout\\\")  // substring match\\n632\\t//\\n633\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\\\"}`)\\n634\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"timeout\\\")  // substring match\\n635\\t//\\n636\\t// # Server Errors\\n637\\t//\\n638\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"error\\\": \\\"Internal server error\\\"}`)\\n639\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"internal.*error\\\")  // regex match\\n640\\t//\\n641\\t//\\tresponse := []byte(`{\\\"message\\\": \\\"Service temporarily unavailable\\\"}`)\\n642\\t//\\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \\\"unavailable\\\")  // substring match\\n643\\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\\n644\\t\\t// Validate inputs\\n645\\t\\tif len(response) == 0 {\\n646\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"response body is empty\\\")\\n647\\t\\t}\\n648\\t\\n649\\t\\tif expectedPattern == \\\"\\\" {\\n650\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"expected pattern cannot be empty\\\")\\n651\\t\\t}\\n652\\t\\n653\\t\\t// Parse JSON body\\n654\\t\\tvar body map[string]interface{}\\n655\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\\n656\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse response body: %w\\\", err)\\n657\\t\\t}\\n658\\t\\n659\\t\\t// Search for error messages in common fields\\n660\\t\\tdefaultFields := []string{\\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\", \\\"detail\\\", \\\"description\\\", \\\"error_description\\\"}\\n661\\t\\tvar foundMessages []string\\n662\\t\\n663\\t\\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\\n664\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\\n665\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \\\"\\\" {\\n666\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\\n667\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n668\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check nested error objects\\n669\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n670\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\\\"message\\\"]; exists {\\n671\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \\\"\\\" {\\n672\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\\n673\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n674\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n675\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n676\\t\\t\\t}\\n677\\t\\t}\\n678\\t\\n679\\t\\t// If no error messages found, return error with response snippet\\n680\\t\\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\\n681\\t\\t\\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\\n682\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"no error message found in response body. Response snippet: %s\\\", snippet)\\n683\\t\\t}\\n684\\t\\n685\\t\\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\\n686\\t\\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\\n687\\t\\n688\\t\\t// Check each found message for pattern match\\n689\\t\\tfor _, message := range foundMessages {\\n690\\t\\t\\tvar matched bool\\n691\\t\\n692\\t\\t\\tif isRegex {\\n693\\t\\t\\t\\t// Try regex matching\\n694\\t\\t\\t\\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\\n695\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n696\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid regex pattern '%s': %w\\\", expectedPattern, err)\\n697\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n698\\t\\t\\t\\tmatched = re.MatchString(message)\\n699\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n700\\t\\t\\t\\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\\n701\\t\\t\\t\\tmatched = strings.Contains(message, expectedPattern)\\n702\\t\\t\\t}\\n703\\t\\n704\\t\\t\\tif matched {\\n705\\t\\t\\t\\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\\n706\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n707\\t\\t\\t}\\n708\\t\\t}\\n709\\t\\n710\\t\\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error\\n711\\t\\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\\n712\\t\\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\\n713\\t\\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\\n714\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"pattern '%s' not found in error messages. First message: \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\". Response snippet: %s\\\",\\n715\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\\n716\\t\\t}\\n717\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"pattern '%s' not found in error message: \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\". Response snippet: %s\\\",\\n718\\t\\t\\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\\n719\\t}\\n720\\t\\n721\\t// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\\n722\\t// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\\n723\\tfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\\n724\\t\\tregexChars := []string{\\\".\\\", \\\"*\\\", \\\"+\\\", \\\"?\\\", \\\"^\\\", \\\"$\\\", \\\"{\\\", \\\"}\\\", \\\"[\\\", \\\"]\\\", \\\"(\\\", \\\")\\\", \\\"|\\\", \\\"\\\\\\\\\\\"}\\n725\\t\\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\\n726\\t\\t\\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\\n727\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n728\\t\\t\\t}\\n729\\t\\t}\\n730\\t\\treturn false\\n731\\t}\\n732\\t\\n733\\t// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\\n734\\t// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\\n735\\tfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\\n736\\t\\t// Convert to string and truncate\\n737\\t\\tresponseStr := string(response)\\n738\\t\\tmaxLen := 200\\n739\\t\\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\\n740\\t\\t\\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \\\"...\\\"\\n741\\t\\t}\\n742\\t\\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\\n743\\t\\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \\\"\\\\n\\\", \\\" \\\")\\n744\\t\\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \\\"\\\\r\\\", \\\" \\\")\\n745\\t\\treturn responseStr\\n746\\t}\\n747\\t\\n748\\t// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\\n749\\t//\\n750\\t// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\\n751\\t// - Custom field selection\\n752\\t// - Case-insensitive matching\\n753\\t// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\\n754\\t// - Multiple pattern checking\\n755\\t//\\n756\\t// Parameters:\\n757\\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\\n758\\t//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\\n759\\t//\\n760\\t// Returns:\\n761\\t//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\\n762\\t//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\\n763\\t//\\n764\\t// Example usage:\\n765\\t//\\n766\\t//\\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\\n767\\t//\\t    Pattern:         \\\"unauthorized\\\",\\n768\\t//\\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\\n769\\t//\\t    FieldNames:      []string{\\\"error\\\", \\\"detail\\\"},\\n770\\t//\\t}\\n771\\t//\\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\\n772\\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\\n773\\t\\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\\n774\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"response body is empty\\\")\\n775\\t\\t}\\n776\\t\\n777\\t\\tif config == nil {\\n778\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"config cannot be nil\\\")\\n779\\t\\t}\\n780\\t\\n781\\t\\tif config.Pattern == \\\"\\\" {\\n782\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"pattern cannot be empty\\\")\\n783\\t\\t}\\n784\\t\\n785\\t\\t// Parse JSON body\\n786\\t\\tvar body map[string]interface{}\\n787\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\\n788\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse response body: %w\\\", err)\\n789\\t\\t}\\n790\\t\\n791\\t\\t// Compile regex pattern\\n792\\t\\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\\n793\\t\\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\\n794\\t\\t\\tregexPattern = \\\"(?i)\\\" + config.Pattern\\n795\\t\\t}\\n796\\t\\n797\\t\\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\\n798\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n799\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid regex pattern: %w\\\", err)\\n800\\t\\t}\\n801\\t\\n802\\t\\t// Determine which fields to check\\n803\\t\\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\\n804\\t\\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\\n805\\t\\t\\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\\\"error\\\", \\\"message\\\", \\\"detail\\\", \\\"description\\\"}\\n806\\t\\t}\\n807\\t\\n808\\t\\t// Check each specified field\\n809\\t\\tmatchedFields := []string{}\\n810\\t\\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\\n811\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\\n812\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \\\"\\\" {\\n813\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\\n814\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\\n815\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif config.MatchAny {\\n816\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n817\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n818\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n819\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n820\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check nested error objects\\n821\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n822\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\\\"message\\\"]; exists {\\n823\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \\\"\\\" {\\n824\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\\n825\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\\n826\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif config.MatchAny {\\n827\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n828\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n829\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n830\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n831\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n832\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n833\\t\\t\\t}\\n834\\t\\t}\\n835\\t\\n836\\t\\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\\n837\\t\\tif !config.MatchAny {\\n838\\t\\t\\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\\n839\\t\\t}\\n840\\t\\n841\\t\\treturn false, nil\\n842\\t}\\n843\\t\\n844\\t// =============================================================================\\n845\\t// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\\n846\\t// =============================================================================\\n847\\t\\n848\\t// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\\n849\\t//\\n850\\t// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\\n851\\t// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\\n852\\t//\\n853\\t// Parameters:\\n854\\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\\n855\\t//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \\\"INVALID_TOKEN\\\", \\\"AUTH_FAILED\\\")\\n856\\t//\\n857\\t// Returns:\\n858\\t//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\\n859\\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\\n860\\t//\\n861\\t// Example usage:\\n862\\t//\\n863\\t//\\t// Check for \\\"AUTH_FAILED\\\" error code\\n864\\t//\\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \\\"AUTH_FAILED\\\")\\n865\\t//\\n866\\t//\\t// Check for OAuth \\\"invalid_token\\\" error\\n867\\t//\\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \\\"invalid_token\\\")\\n868\\tfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\\n869\\t\\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\\n870\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"response body is empty\\\")\\n871\\t\\t}\\n872\\t\\n873\\t\\tif expectedCode == \\\"\\\" {\\n874\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"expected code cannot be empty\\\")\\n875\\t\\t}\\n876\\t\\n877\\t\\t// Parse JSON body\\n878\\t\\tvar body map[string]interface{}\\n879\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\\n880\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse response body: %w\\\", err)\\n881\\t\\t}\\n882\\t\\n883\\t\\t// Common error code field names\\n884\\t\\terrorCodeFields := []string{\\\"error_code\\\", \\\"errorCode\\\", \\\"code\\\", \\\"error\\\", \\\"status\\\"}\\n885\\t\\n886\\t\\t// Check for error code in standard fields\\n887\\t\\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\\n888\\t\\t\\tif expectedCode) {\\n892\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n893\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n894\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n895\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check for nested error code in error object\\n896\\t\\t\\t\\tif nestedObj, ok := {\\n899\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\\n900\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n901\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n902\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n903\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n904\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n905\\t\\t\\t}\\n906\\t\\t}\\n907\\t\\n908\\t\\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\\n909\\t\\tif errorCode, exists := body[\\\"error\\\"]; exists {\\n910\\t\\t\\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\\n911\\t\\t\\t\\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\\n912\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n913\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n914\\t\\t\\t}\\n915\\t\\t}\\n916\\t\\n917\\t\\treturn false, nil\\n918\\t}\\n919\\t\\n920\\t// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\\n921\\t//\\n922\\t// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\\n923\\t// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\\n924\\t// information are consistent.\\n925\\t//\\n926\\t// Parameters:\\n927\\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\\n928\\t//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP - error if body parsing fails\\n934\\t//\\n935\\t// Example usage:\\n936\\t//\\n937\\t//\\t// Validate 401 response with \\\"UNAUTHORIZED\\\" error code\\n938\\t//\\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 401, \\\"UNAUTHORIZED\\\")\\n939\\t//\\n940\\t//\\t// Validate 403 response with \\\"FORBIDDEN\\\" error code\\n941\\t//\\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 403, \\\"FORBIDDEN\\\")\\n942\\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\\n943\\t\\tif {\\n949\\t\\t\\treturn false, nil\\n950\\t\\t}\\n951\\t\\n952\\t\\t// Read response body\\n953\\t\\tvar bodyBytes []byte\\n954\\t\\tif resp.Body != nil {\\n955\\t\\t\\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\\n956\\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n957\\t\\t\\tbodyBytes, err = io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\\n958\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n959\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to read response body: %w\\\", err)\\n960\\t\\t\\t}\\n961\\t\\t}\\n962\\t\\n963\\t\\t// Check error code\\n964\\t\\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\\n965\\t\\tif err != nil pattern.Pattern)\\n1461\\t\\t\\t}\\n1462\\t\\t\\tresult.PatternMatched = regex.MatchString(foundMessage)\\n1463\\t\\t\\tif !result.PatternMatched {\\n1464\\t\\t\\t\\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"message does not match required pattern: %s\\\", pattern.Pattern))\\n1465\\t\\t\\t}\\n1466\\t\\t} else {\\n1467\\t\\t\\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\\n1468\\t\\t}\\n1469\\t\\n1470\\t\\t// Validate MustContain strings\\n1471\\t\\tallMustContainFound := true\\n1472\\t\\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\\n1473\\t\\t\\tvar found bool\\n1474\\t\\t\\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\\n1475\\t\\t\\t\\t// Case-insensitive search\\n1476\\t\\t\\t\\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\\n1477\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1478\\t\\t\\t\\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\\n1479\\t\\t\\t}\\n1480\\t\\t\\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\\n1481\\t\\t\\tif !found {\\n1482\\t\\t\\t\\tallMustContainFound = false\\n1483\\t\\t\\t\\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"message does not contain required string: %s\\\", mustContain))\\n1484\\t\\t\\t}\\n1485\\t\\t}\\n1486\\t\\n1487\\t\\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\\n1488\\t\\thasForbiddenStrings := false\\n1489\\t\\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\\n1490\\t\\t\\tvar found bool\\n1491\\t\\t\\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\\n1492\\t\\t\\t\\t// Case-insensitive search\\n1493\\t\\t\\t\\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\\n1494\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1495\\t\\t\\t\\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\\n1496\\t\\t\\t}\\n1497\\t\\t\\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\\n1498\\t\\t\\tif found {\\n1499\\t\\t\\t\\thasForbiddenStrings = true\\n1500\\t\\t\\t\\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"message contains forbidden string: %s\\\", mustNotContain))\\n1501\\t\\t\\t}\\n1502\\t\\t}\\n1503\\t\\n1504\\t\\t// Overall validity\\n1505\\t\\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\\n1506\\t\\t\\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\\n1507\\t\\n1508\\t\\treturn result\\n1509\\t}\\n1510\\t\\n1511\\t// =============================================================================\\n1512\\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\\n1513\\t// =============================================================================\\n1514\\t\\n1515\\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\\n1516\\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\\n1517\\t\\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error code was found\\n1518\\t\\tFieldName string\\n1519\\t\\t// CodeValue is the actual error code value (as a string for flexibility)\\n1520\\t\\tCodeValue string\\n1521\\t\\t// NumericCode is the error code parsed as an integer (if applicable)\\n1522\\t\\tNumericCode *int\\n1523\\t\\t// MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched this code\\n1524\\t\\tMatchedPattern string\\n1525\\t\\t// Position describes where in the response the code was found\\n1526\\t\\tPosition string\\n1527\\t}\\n1528\\t\\n1529\\t// EnhancedErrorCodePattern defines a pattern for finding error codes in responses.\\n1530\\ttype EnhancedErrorCodePattern struct {\\n1531\\t\\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check for error codes\\n1532\\t\\tFieldNames []string\\n1533\\t\\t// NumericOnly indicates whether to only match numeric error codes\\n1534\\t\\tNumericOnly bool\\n1535\\t\\t// ValuePatterns contains regex patterns for valid error code values\\n1536\\t\\tValuePatterns []string\\n1537\\t}\\n1538\\t\\n1539\\t// FindErrorCodesInResponse searches for error codes in an HTTP response body.\\n1540\\t// This is useful for validating that specific error codes are present in error responses.\\n1541\\t//\\n1542\\t// Parameters:\\n1543\\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\\n1544\\t//   - patterns: The patterns to use for finding error codes\\n1545\\t//\\n1546\\t// Returns a slice of ErrorCodeMatch representing all found error codes.\\n1547\\t//\\n1548\\t// Example usage:\\n1549\\t//\\n1550\\t//\\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\\n1551\\t//\\tpatterns := []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\\n1552\\t//\\t    {\\n1553\\t//\\t        FieldNames: []string{\\\"error_code\\\", \\\"code\\\"},\\n1554\\t//\\t        NumericOnly: true,\\n1555\\t//\\t    },\\n1556\\t//\\t    {\\n1557\\t//\\t        FieldNames: []string{\\\"error\\\"},\\n1558\\t//\\t        ValuePatterns: []string{\\\"invalid_token\\\", \\\"access_denied\\\"},\\n1559\\t//\\t    },\\n1560\\t//\\t}\\n1561\\t//\\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(body, patterns)\\n1562\\t//\\tfor _, match := range matches {\\n1563\\t//\\t    log.Printf(\\\"Found error code: %s in field: %s\\\", match.CodeValue, match.FieldName)\\n1564\\t//\\t}\\n1565\\tfunc FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody interface{}, patterns []EnhancedErrorCodePattern) []ErrorCodeMatch {\\n1566\\t\\tvar matches []ErrorCodeMatch\\n1567\\t\\n1568\\t\\tif responseBody == nil {\\n1569\\t\\t\\treturn matches\\n1570\\t\\t}\\n1571\\t\\n1572\\t\\t// Try to convert to map\\n1573\\t\\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\\n1574\\t\\tif !ok {\\n1575\\t\\t\\treturn matches\\n1576\\t\\t}\\n1577\\t\\n1578\\t\\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\\n1579\\t\\t\\t// Default to common error code fields if none specified\\n1580\\t\\t\\tfieldsToCheck := pattern.FieldNames\\n1581\\t\\t\\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\\n1582\\t\\t\\t\\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\\\"error_code\\\", \\\"code\\\", \\\"error\\\", \\\"status\\\"}\\n1583\\t\\t\\t}\\n1584\\t\\n1585\\t\\t\\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\\n1586\\t\\t\\t\\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\\n1587\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvar codeValue string\\n1588\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvar numericCode *int\\n1589\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvar matchedPattern string\\n1590\\t\\n1591\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Handle different value types\\n1592\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tswitch v := value.(type) {\\n1593\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase string:\\n1594\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = v\\n1595\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"string\\\"\\n1596\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Try to parse as number\\n1597\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif num, err := parseIntFromString(v); err == nil && pattern.NumericOnly {\\n1598\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnumericCode = &num\\n1599\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1600\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase int:\\n1601\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d\\\", v)\\n1602\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnumericCode = &v\\n1603\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"numeric\\\"\\n1604\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase float64:\\n1605\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// JSON numbers are often parsed as float64\\n1606\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\\n1607\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnum := int(v)\\n1608\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%d\\\", num)\\n1609\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnumericCode = &num\\n1610\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"numeric\\\"\\n1611\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1612\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase bool:\\n1613\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Some APIs use true/false as error codes\\n1614\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif v {\\n1615\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = \\\"true\\\"\\n1616\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"boolean\\\"\\n1617\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1618\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcodeValue = \\\"false\\\"\\n1619\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = \\\"boolean\\\"\\n1620\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1621\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n1622\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\\n1623\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1624\\t\\n1625\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\\n1626\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched := true\\n1627\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\\n1628\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched = matched {\\n1632\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched = true\\n1633\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\\n1634\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n1635\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1636\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1637\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1638\\t\\n1639\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check numeric-only constraint\\n1640\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\\n1641\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatternsMatched = false\\n1642\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1643\\t\\n1644\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif patternsMatched && codeValue != \\\"\\\" {\\n1645\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatches = append(matches, ErrorCodeMatch{\\n1646\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tFieldName:      fieldName,\\n1647\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tCodeValue:      codeValue,\\n1648\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\\n1649\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\\n1650\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\\\"field '%s' in matches\\n1658\\t}\\n1659\\t\\n1660\\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\\n1661\\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\\n1678\\t\\tif responseBody == nil {\\n1679\\t\\t\\treturn []string{fieldName},\\n1686\\t\\t\\t}\\n1687\\t\\t} else {\\n1688\\t\\t\\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\\n1689\\t\\t\\t\\tFieldNames: []string{\\\"error_code\\\", \\\"code\\\", \\\"error\\\", \\\"status\\\"},\\n1690\\t\\t\\t}\\n1691\\t\\t}\\n1692\\t\\n1693\\t\\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody, []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{pattern})\\n1694\\t\\tfor _, match := range matches {\\n1695\\t\\t\\tif match.CodeValue == expectedCode {\\n1696\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n1697\\t\\t\\t}\\n1698\\t\\t}\\n1699\\t\\n1700\\t\\treturn false\\n1701\\t}\\n1702\\t\\n1703\\t// parseIntFromString attempts to parse a string as an integer.\\n1704\\tfunc parseIntFromString(s string) (int, error) {\\n1705\\t\\tvar result int\\n1706\\t\\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \\\"%d\\\", &result)\\n1707\\t\\treturn result, err\\n1708\\t}\\n1709\\t\\n1710\\t// =============================================================================\\n1711\\t// STATUS CODE RANGE VALIDATION (PATTERN-BASED)\\n1712\\t// =============================================================================\\n1713\\t\\n1714\\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt validates a status code against a pattern string.\\n1715\\t// It supports patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx' to check if a status code\\n1716\\t// falls within a specified range.\\n1717\\t//\\n1718\\t// Parameters:\\n1719\\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\\n1720\\t//   - actual: The actual status code to validate\\n1721\\t//\\n1722\\t// Returns error if the pattern is invalid or if the actual code doesn't match the range.\\n1723\\t// Returns nil if the actual code falls within the specified range.\\n1724\\t//\\n1725\\t// Example usage:\\n1726\\t//\\n1727\\t//\\t// Validate 404 is in 4xx range\\n1728\\t//\\terr := ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\\\"4xx\\\", 404)\\n1729\\t//\\tif err != nil {\\n1730\\t//\\t    log.Printf(\\\"Status validation failed: %v\\\", err)\\n1731\\t//\\t}\\n1732\\t//\\n1733\\t//\\t// Validate 500 is in 5xx range\\n1734\\t//\\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\\\"5xx\\\", 500)\\n1735\\t//\\n1736\\t//\\t// This returns an error because 200 is not in 4xx range\\n1737\\t//\\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\\\"4xx\\\", 200)\\n1738\\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(pattern string, actual int) error {\\n1739\\t\\t// Validate pattern format\\n1740\\t\\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\\n1741\\t\\t\\treturn FormatValidationError(\\n1742\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"status_code_range\\\",\\n1743\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"pattern in format 'Nxx' (3 chars)\\\"),\\n1744\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"'%s' (%d chars)\\\", pattern, len(pattern)),\\n1745\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid pattern format: %s\\\", pattern),\\n1746\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"\\\",\\n1747\\t\\t\\t)\\n1748\\t\\t}\\n1749\\t\\n1750\\t\\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\\n1751\\t\\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\\n1752\\t\\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\\n1753\\t\\t\\treturn FormatValidationError(\\n1754\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"status_code_range\\\",\\n1755\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"century digit 1-5\\\",\\n1756\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"'%c' (ASCII %d)\\\", centuryChar, centuryChar),\\n1757\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid pattern century in '%s'\\\", pattern),\\n1758\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"\\\",\\n1759\\t\\t\\t)\\n1760\\t\\t}\\n1761\\t\\n1762\\t\\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\\n1763\\t\\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\\n1764\\t\\t\\treturn FormatValidationError(\\n1765\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"status_code_range\\\",\\n1766\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"pattern ending with 'xx'\\\",\\n1767\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"'%s'\\\", pattern[1:]),\\n1768\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid pattern suffix in '%s'\\\", pattern),\\n1769\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"\\\",\\n1770\\t\\t\\t)\\n1771\\t\\t}\\n1772\\t\\n1773\\t\\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\\n1774\\t\\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\\n1775\\t\\tminRange := century * 100\\n1776\\t\\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\\n1777\\t\\n1778\\t\\t// Get description for the {\\n1783\\t\\t\\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s (%d-%d)\\\", pattern, minRange, maxRange)\\n1784\\t\\t\\tif desc != \\\"\\\" {\\n1785\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s %s (%d-%d)\\\", pattern, desc, minRange, maxRange)\\n1786\\t\\t\\t}\\n1787\\t\\n1788\\t\\t\\treturn FormatValidationError(\\n1789\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"status_code_range\\\",\\n1790\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedRange,\\n1791\\t\\t\\t\\tactual,\\n1792\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"status code validation failed for pattern '%s'\\\", pattern),\\n1793\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"\\\",\\n1794\\t\\t\\t)\\n1795\\t\\t}\\n1796\\t\\n1797\\t\\treturn nil\\n1798\\t}\\n1799\\t\\n1800\\t// ParseStatusCodeRange extracts the century digit and range from a pattern string.\\n1801\\t// This is a helper function for advanced use cases where you need the range bounds.\\n1802\\t//\\n1803\\t// Parameters:\\n1804\\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\\n1805\\t//\\n1806\\t// Returns the min and max values of the range, or an error if the pattern is invalid.\\n1807\\t//\\n1808\\t// Example {\\n1814\\t//\\t    fmt.Printf(\\\"Range: %d-%d\\\\n\\\", min, max)\\n1815\\t//\\t}\\n1816\\tfunc ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern string) (min int, max int, err error) {\\n1817\\t\\t// Validate pattern format\\n1818\\t\\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\\n1819\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid pattern format: %s (expected format: '4xx', '5xx', etc.)\\\", pattern)\\n1820\\t\\t}\\n1821\\t\\n1822\\t\\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\\n1823\\t\\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\\n1824\\t\\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' nil\\n1839\\t}\\n1840\\t\\n1841\\t// GetStatusCodeRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range pattern.\\n1842\\t//\\n1843\\t// Parameters:\\n1844\\t//   - pattern: > '5' {\\n1863\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid pattern century: %c\\\", centuryChar)\\n1864\\t\\t}\\n1865\\t\\n1866\\t\\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\\n1867\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid pattern suffix: %s\\\", pattern)\\n1868\\t\\t}\\n1869\\t\\n1870\\t\\tdescriptions := map[byte]string{\\n1871\\t\\t\\t'1': \\\"Informational (1xx)\\\",\\n1872\\t\\t\\t'2': \\\"Success (2xx)\\\",\\n1873\\t\\t\\t'3': \\\"Redirection (3xx)\\\",\\n1874\\t\\t\\t'4': \\\"Client Error (4xx)\\\",\\n1875\\t\\t\\t'5': \\\"Server Error (5xx)\\\",\\n1876\\t\\t}\\n1877\\t\\n1878\\t\\tdesc, ok := descriptions[centuryChar]\\n1879\\t\\tif !ok {\\n1880\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown century: %c\\\", centuryChar)\\n1881\\t\\t}\\n1882\\t\\n1883\\t\\treturn desc, nil\\n1884\\t}\\n1885\\t\\n1886\\t// =============================================================================\\n1887\\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING VALIDATION ERRORS\\n1888\\t// =============================================================================\\n1889\\t\\n1890\\t// FormatValidationError creates a standardized ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\\n1891\\t//\\n1892\\t// This helper function automatically generates relevant suggestions based on the\\n1893\\t// validation type and the mismatch between expected and actual values.\\n1894\\t//\\n1895\\t// Parameters:\\n1896\\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \\\"status_code\\\", \\\"error_message\\\")\\n1897\\t//   - expected: The expected value\\n1898\\t//   - actual: The actual value received\\n1899\\t//   - context: Additional context (optional)\\n1900\\t//   - responseSnippet: Excerpt from response (optional)\\n1901\\t//   - customSuggestions: Optional custom suggestions (if provided, these override auto-generated suggestions)\\n1902\\t//\\n1903\\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\\n1904\\t//\\n1905\\t// Example usage with auto-generated suggestions:\\n1906\\t//\\n1907\\t//\\terr := FormatValidationError(\\\"status_code\\\", 200, 404, \\\"GET /api/users\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n1908\\t//\\n1909\\t// Example usage with custom suggestions:\\n1910\\t//\\n1911\\t//\\terr := FormatValidationError(\\\"status_code\\\", 200, 404, \\\"GET /api/users\\\", \\\"\\\",\\n1912\\t//\\t    \\\"Check the endpoint documentation\\\", \\\"Verify the API version\\\")\\n1913\\tfunc FormatValidationError(validationType string, expected, actual interface{}, context, responseSnippet string, customSuggestions ...string) ValidationError {\\n1914\\t\\tsuggestions := customSuggestions\\n1915\\t\\tif len(customSuggestions) == 0 {\\n1916\\t\\t\\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(validationType, expected, actual)\\n1917\\t\\t}\\n1918\\t\\n1919\\t\\tve := ValidationError{\\n1920\\t\\t\\tErrorType:        validationType,\\n1921\\t\\t\\tMessage:          generateMessageFromParts(validationType, expected, actual),\\n1922\\t\\t\\tExpected:         expected,\\n1923\\t\\t\\tActual:           actual,\\n1924\\t\\t\\tContext:          context,\\n1925\\t\\t\\tResponseSnippet:  responseSnippet,\\n1926\\t\\t\\tSuggestions:      suggestions,\\n1927\\t\\t}\\n1928\\t\\n1929\\t\\treturn ve\\n1930\\t}\\n1931\\t\\n1932\\t// FormatValidationErrorWithDetails creates a ValidationError with extended field support.\\n1933\\t//\\n1934\\t// This function provides full customization over all ValidationError fields, including\\n1935\\t// optional fields like FieldName, Location, RelatedFields, PatternDetails, RangeInfo, ValidationDetails, and custom Suggestions.\\n1936\\t// Use this when you need more detailed error information than FormatValidationError provides.\\n1937\\t//\\n1938\\t// Parameters:\\n1939\\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \\\"status_code\\\", \\\"error_message\\\", \\\"content_type\\\")\\n1940\\t//   - expected: The expected value\\n1941\\t//   - actual: The actual value received\\n1942\\t//   - context: Additional context about the validation =============================================================================\n129\t\n130\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n131\t//\n132\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n192\t//\n193\t// Parameters:\n194\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n195\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n196\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n197\t//\n198\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n272\t//\n273\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n274\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n275\t// your specific requirements.\n276\t//\n277\t// Parameters:\n278\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n279\t//   - expected: The expected value\n280\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n281\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n282\t//\n283\t// Example usage:\n284\t//\n285\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n286\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n287\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n288\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n289\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n290\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n292\t//\t)\n293\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n294\t\tvalidationType string,\n295\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n296\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n297\t) ValidationError {\n298\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n299\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n300\t\t\topt(config)\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n304\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n305\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n306\t\n307\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n308\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n309\t\t}\n310\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n311\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n312\t\t}\n313\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n314\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n315\t\t}\n316\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n317\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n318\t\t}\n319\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n320\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n321\t\t}\n322\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n323\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n324\t\t}\n325\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n326\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n327\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\t// =============================================================================\n333\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n334\t// =============================================================================\n335\t\n336\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n337\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n338\t\tContext            string\n339\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n340\t\tFieldName          string\n341\t\tPatternDetails     string\n342\t\tRangeInfo          string\n343\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n344\t\tSuggestions        []string\n345\t}\n346\t\n347\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n348\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n349\t\n350\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n351\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n352\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n353\t\t\tc.Context = context\n354\t\t}\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n358\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n359\t\treturn func(c
